   152	      ├─ no defined gravitational charge/mass
   153	      └─ action-dependent Noether/Gauss/quasilocal boundary quantities
   154	          └─ weak/local readout only after exact finite-cell equation
   155	```
   156	
   157	The global bootstrap and \(X_{\max}\) sit outside this local fork as **WORKING / POSIT** global selection data. They cannot close any local branch without independent global equations, a noncircular mass definition, a proper-volume definition, and enough closure conditions.
   158	
   159	# D2 — Derivation
   160	
   161	## D2.1 Kinematic content
   162	
   163	**Step K1 — FORCED-BY(C1 §1.2, dual UDT Reciprocity).** For positive diagonal \(P\),
   164	
   165	\[
   166	P^TKP=\begin{pmatrix}0&uv\\uv&0\end{pmatrix}=K
   167	\quad\Longrightarrow\quad uv=1.
   168	\]
   169	
   170	Reciprocal-c alone does not force this; the founding dual-action premise does. The nontrivial ordinary-covariant alternative \(u=v\) fails the full dual ledger because its intersection with \(uv=1\), for positive maps, is only \(u=v=1\).
   171	
   172	**Step K2 — FORCED-BY(C1 §§1.1–2: difference, composition, positivity, and explicit regularity), CONDITIONAL on that regularity.** A regular positive homomorphism from additive depth to multiplication has
   173	
   174	\[
   175	u(\Delta)=e^{a\Delta},\qquad v(\Delta)=e^{b\Delta}.
   176	\]
   177	
   178	The relation \(uv=1\) gives \(a+b=0\). **CHOSEN(sign/unit convention, alternatives retained):** write the parameter as \(\phi=-a\Delta\), so
   179	
   180	\[
   181	P(\phi)=\operatorname{diag}(e^{-\phi},e^{\phi}).
   182	\]
   183	
   184	The theorem does not derive the existence/founding status of positional dilation, regularity, the physical unit of \(\phi\), or a metric.
   185	
   186	**Step K3 — FORCED-BY(C1 §1.3) as algebra, with the physical neutrality itself FOUNDING.** Any positive diagonal comparison decomposes as
   187	
   188	\[
   189	\operatorname{diag}(u,v)=\sqrt{uv}\,
   190	\operatorname{diag}\!\left(e^{-\phi},e^\phi\right),
   191	\qquad \phi={1\over2}\log(v/u).
   192	\]
   193	
   194	CSN declares the first factor calibration. This does not choose a gauge globally.
   195	
   196	**Step K4 — CHOSEN(C1 §2: Lorentzian readout, representative, sign/unit; alternatives retained).** In the displayed representative,
   197	
   198	\[
   199	ds^2_{(2)}=-e^{-2\phi}c^2dt^2+e^{2\phi}d\ell_\parallel^2,
   200	\quad (-g_{tt}/c^2)g_{\parallel\parallel}=1,
   201	\quad \det g_{(2)}=-c^2.
   202	\]
   203	
   204	No equation for \(\phi\) follows from this identity.
   205	
   206	## D2.2 Q1: action and boundary countermodel gate
   207	
   208	**Step A1 — FORCED-BY(absence stated in C1 §§1.3, 2 and the countermodel rule C0 §2 rules 11–12): the kinematic ledger does not select an action principle.** This is not merely an argument from silence: mutually distinct constructions obey the supplied kinematics.
   209	
   210	- **CM0 (no-action branch):** take the kinematic configuration space K0/K2 with no variational functional. The founding relations remain kinematic constraints/readout data. Because C0/C1 never posit stationarity, CM0 is admissible. Therefore even the existence of an action is not forced.
   211	- **CMW (local metric, fourth derivative): CHOSEN(CSN-respecting locality and four-dimensional covariance).** On a declared conformal-metric branch,
   212	
   213	  \[
   214	  S_W=\alpha\int_M d^4x\sqrt{-g}\,C_{\mu\nu\rho\sigma}C^{\mu\nu\rho\sigma},
   215	  \]
   216	
   217	  where \(\alpha\) is a free dimensionless coefficient. The exact bulk equation for unrestricted conformal metric variations is \(B_{\mu\nu}=0\), with
   218	
   219	  \[
   220	  B_{\mu\nu}=(\nabla^\rho\nabla^\sigma+\tfrac12R^{\rho\sigma})C_{\mu\rho\nu\sigma}
   221	  \]
   222	
   223	  up to the displayed curvature-sign convention. Equivalently, this equation is fixed convention-independently by defining \(\delta S_W=-2\alpha\int\sqrt{-g}\,B_{\mu\nu}\delta g^{\mu\nu}+\delta S_{W,\partial}\). It is fourth order. Kinematic block enforcement still requires K0 restriction or a K1 constraint; the action does not derive it.
   224	- **CMW+4 (local metric plus depth, distinct nonlinear operator): CHOSEN(CSN-respecting locality, covariance, and independent weight-zero \(\phi\)).** Put \(X=g^{\mu\nu}\nabla_\mu\phi\nabla_\nu\phi\) and
   225	
   226	  \[
   227	  S=S_W-{a\over4}\int_M\sqrt{-g}\,X^2+S_m[g,\phi,\psi].
   228	  \]
   229	
   230	  In four dimensions \(\sqrt{-g}X^2\) is CSN invariant if \(\phi\) has Weyl weight zero. Define
   231	
   232	  \[
   233	  J_\phi={1\over\sqrt{-g}}{\delta S_m\over\delta\phi},
   234	  \qquad \rho_\phi=-J_\phi.
   235	  \]
   236	
   237	  Varying before reduction gives the exact nonlinear scalar equation and scalar boundary variation
   238	
   239	  \[
   240	  a\nabla_\mu(X\nabla^\mu\phi)=\rho_\phi,
   241	  \qquad
   242	  \delta S_{\phi,\partial}=-a\int_{\partial M}\sqrt{|\gamma|}\,
   243	       X n^\mu\nabla_\mu\phi\,\delta\phi.
   244	  \]
   245	
   246	  Its stress tensor is exactly
   247	
   248	  \[
   249	  T^{(\phi)}_{\mu\nu}=aX\nabla_\mu\phi\nabla_\nu\phi
   250	       -{a\over4}g_{\mu\nu}X^2,
   251	  \]
   252	
   253	  which is trace-free in four dimensions. With \(B_{\mu\nu}\) defined by the variation above and total non-Weyl-sector stress defined by \(\delta(S_\phi+S_m)=-\frac12\int\sqrt{-g}T_{\mu\nu}\delta g^{\mu\nu}\), the exact metric equation is
   254	
   255	  \[
   256	  4\alpha B_{\mu\nu}+T_{\mu\nu}=0.
   257	  \]
   258	
   259	  A Weyl-consistent matter sector would also have to be chosen; none is supplied. Setting \(S_m=0\) is a complete vacuum countermodel. The constants \(a,\alpha\), their signs, and their ratio are **FREE / CHOSE**, not UDT-derived.
   260	
   261	CM0, CMW, and CMW+4 differ in operator existence, field census, and equations while respecting the same frozen kinematics. Other local conformal invariants, topological actions, and nonlocal conformal functionals enlarge the family. Consequently no uniqueness claim survives.
   262	
   263	**Step A2 — boundary functional is UNDERDETERMINED.** The canon fixes \(\phi=0\) at the static seal, so allowed static variations have \(\delta\phi=0\) there and the displayed first-derivative scalar boundary variation vanishes without fixing \(n\cdot\nabla\phi\). This proves compatibility for that chosen branch, not selection of its action. CMW has higher-derivative boundary variations and requires additional data or boundary terms. Adding a four-dimensional Euler density with its associated boundary completion changes the finite-cell action/boundary accounting without changing local bulk equations. Arbitrary admissible conformal boundary functionals provide further countermodels. The canon does not select among Dirichlet, mixed, higher-derivative, corner, lapse, shift, or carrier data, apart from the stated static \(\phi\) value.
   264	
   265	## D2.3 Q2: carrier and four-dimensional completion
   266	
   267	**Step C1 — FORCED-BY(C1 §4): retain a carrier-agnostic branch.** No carrier, coupling, stress, or source is derivable there. A matter-free geometry and any otherwise admissible carrier class remain live.
   268	
   269	**Step C2 — CHOSEN(CONDITIONAL round-\(S^2\) carrier and four-dimensional covariance; alternatives retained).** One particular completion is
   270	
   271	\[
   272	S_n=\int_M\sqrt{-g}\,d^4x\left[-{\xi\over2}\nabla_\mu\mathbf n\!\cdot\!\nabla^\mu\mathbf n
   273	-{\kappa_4\over4}F_{\mu\nu}F^{\mu\nu}
   274	+\lambda(\mathbf n^2-1)\right],
   275	\]
   276	
   277	\[
   278	F_{\mu\nu}=\mathbf n\cdot(\nabla_\mu\mathbf n\times\nabla_\nu\mathbf n).
   279	\]
   280	
   281	This measure, signature \((-+++)\), contraction, constraint implementation, and minimality are all **CHOSE**. In flat space with \(\partial_t\mathbf n=0\), \(S_n\) per unit time is exactly \(-E\), so it is a completion of the supplied energy.
   282	
   283	Let \(\Pi=I-\mathbf n\mathbf n^T\) project tangent to \(S^2\). Varying the full four-dimensional action first gives the exact nonlinear carrier equation
   284	
   285	\[
   286	\Pi\left[\xi\,\Box\mathbf n
   287	-\kappa_4(\nabla_\mu F^{\mu\nu})(\mathbf n\times\nabla_\nu\mathbf n)\right]=0,
   288	\qquad \mathbf n^2=1,
   289	\]
   290	
   291	with the normal component absorbed by \(\lambda\). An equivalent projected form may move derivatives onto \(F^{\mu\nu}(\mathbf n\times\nabla_\nu\mathbf n)\); their difference is normal to the sphere. The exact stress generated by this chosen metric dependence is
   292	
   293	\[
   294	T^{(n)}_{\mu\nu}=\xi\left(\nabla_\mu\mathbf n\cdot\nabla_\nu\mathbf n
   295	-\tfrac12g_{\mu\nu}\nabla_\rho\mathbf n\cdot\nabla^\rho\mathbf n\right)
   296	+\kappa_4\left(F_{\mu\rho}F_\nu{}^\rho-\tfrac14g_{\mu\nu}F_{\rho\sigma}F^{\rho\sigma}\right).
   297	\]
   298	
   299	Its boundary variation contains
   300	
   301	\[
   302	-\int_{\partial M}\sqrt{|\gamma|}\,n_\mu\,
   303	\xi\nabla^\mu\mathbf n\cdot\delta\mathbf n
   304	+\int_{\partial M}\sqrt{|\gamma|}\,n_\mu\,
   305	\kappa_4F^{\mu\nu}\,\delta\mathbf n\cdot
   306	(\mathbf n\times\nabla_\nu\mathbf n),
   307	\]
   308	
   309	so carrier boundary data are required and are not supplied. Neither this stress nor its coupling to a geometry equation is available until this completion and a gravitational action are chosen.
   310	
   311	The quadratic term is not pre-scale CSN invariant with constant \(\xi\): in four dimensions \(\sqrt{-g}g^{\mu\nu}\partial_\mu n\partial_\nu n\) has Weyl weight +2. A native conformal completion can instead introduce a weight \(-1\) compensator \(\chi\), replacing \(\xi\) by \(\chi^2\), plus an independently chosen \(\chi\) action; a gauge/solution \(\chi^2=\xi\) then recovers the displayed coefficient. This adds an unselected field and dynamics. The quartic \(F^2\) density is conformal in four dimensions. Alternatively, the entire minimal action may be used only after a representative/material scale is selected. These are live, inequivalent branches.
   312	
   313	**Step C3 — CHOSEN(foliation completion; alternatives retained).** A second exact completion, written with lapse \(N\), shift through \(D_t=\partial_t-\mathcal L_\beta\), and spatial metric \(h_{ij}\), is
   314	
   315	\[
   316	S_{\rm fol}=\int dt\,d^3x\,N\sqrt h\left[
   317	{a_2\over2N^2}|D_t\mathbf n|^2-{\xi\over2}h^{ij}\partial_i\mathbf n\cdot\partial_j\mathbf n
   318	+{a_4\over2N^2}h^{ij}F_{ti}F_{tj}
   319	-{\kappa_4\over4}h^{ik}h^{jl}F_{ij}F_{kl}\right].
   320	\]
   321	
   322	For zero shift, flat static fields, every \(a_2,a_4\) gives the same supplied energy. Minimal Lorentz covariance would impose the particular choice \(a_2=\xi\), \(a_4=\kappa_4\), but covariance is an added premise. The exact nonlinear field equation of this branch is the constrained Euler–Lagrange operator
   323	
   324	\[
   325	\Pi_A{}^B\left[{\partial L_{\rm fol}\over\partial n^B}
   326	-{1\over N\sqrt h}\partial_\mu\left(N\sqrt h\,
   327	{\partial L_{\rm fol}\over\partial(\partial_\mu n^B)}\right)\right]=0,
   328	\]
   329	
   330	where the displayed \(L_{\rm fol}\) fixes every term, including its nonlinear \(F\)-dependence. This divergence form is the full operator, not a static reduction. Its canonical boundary momentum is \(N\sqrt h\,\partial L_{\rm fol}/\partial(\partial_\mu n^A)\), hence changes with \(a_2,a_4\). Lapse and shift variations also differ from the covariant completion.
   331	
   332	**Step C4 — FORCED-BY(the nonempty \(\ker\mathcal R\) demonstrated above): completion and coupling are UNDERDETERMINED.** Arbitrary terms in \(\ker\mathcal R\), weights \(w_2(\phi),w_4(\phi)\) that reduce appropriately on the test background, different measures, curvature couplings, carrier boundary terms, and nonlocal completions preserve the supplied static energy while changing time evolution and source variation. No weight is forced. A direct \(\phi\)-coupling, metric-only coupling, no coupling, or a conformally compensated coupling are all live. A global density average may not be inserted as a local fitted weight (C1 §6).
   333	
   334	## D2.4 Q3: finite-cell charge and controlled readout
   335	
   336	**Step Q1 — FORCED-BY(C1 §§2–3 and the action countermodels): no native gravitational charge/mass definition is determined.** CM0 has no equation or Noether/Gauss charge. CMW has a different higher-derivative boundary structure from CMW+4. The conditional \(S^2\) stress exists only on the chosen completion. There is no supplied source normalization, \(G\), timelike symmetry, Hamiltonian boundary term, or relation between a boundary flux and proper total mass. Therefore “gravitational mass” cannot be named by relabeling an integral.
   337	
   338	What is fixed is only the finite-cell discipline: any chosen charge is evaluated on an actual finite two-surface in \(\Sigma\), especially the seal, never at spatial infinity.
   339	
   340	**Step Q2 — CHOSEN(CMW+4 as an explicit finite-cell demonstration; alternatives retained).** The exact covariant equation was established before reduction:
   341	
   342	\[
   343	a\nabla_\mu(X\nabla^\mu\phi)=\rho_\phi.
   344	\]
   345	
   346	For a static metric \(ds^2=-N^2c^2dt^2+h_{ij}dx^idx^j\) with zero shift and static \(\phi\), it becomes exactly
   347	
   348	\[
   349	{a\over N\sqrt h}\partial_i\left(N\sqrt h\,Xh^{ij}\partial_j\phi\right)=\rho_\phi,
   350	\qquad X=h^{ij}\partial_i\phi\partial_j\phi.
   351	\]
   352	
   353	For any finite region \(V\subset\Sigma\), Stokes' theorem gives the action-defined charge/flux pair
   354	
   355	\[
   356	Q_\phi[V]\equiv\int_V N\sqrt h\,\rho_\phi\,d^3x
   357	=a\oint_{\partial V}N\sqrt\sigma\,X\,s^i\partial_i\phi\,d^2x.
   358	\]
   359	
   360	This is exact. It is a \(\phi\)-source charge for this chosen action, not a derived gravitational mass. The lapse weight, coefficient \(a\), source definition, and even existence of this Gauss law change on other branches. At the seal, \(\phi=0\) while \(s\cdot\nabla\phi\) is free; hence the canon permits a nonzero flux but fixes neither its value nor its identification.
   361	
   362	**Step Q3 — CHOSEN(static, spherical, diagonal, areal representative after variation; alternatives retained).** Take
   363	
   364	\[
   365	ds^2=-e^{-2\phi(r)}c^2dt^2+e^{2\phi(r)}dr^2+r^2d\Omega^2.
   366	\]
   367	
   368	Then \(N\sqrt h=r^2\sin\theta\), \(X=e^{-2\phi}(\phi')^2\), and the exact reduced equation is
   369	
   370	\[
   371	{a\over r^2}{d\over dr}\left[r^2e^{-4\phi}(\phi')^3\right]=\rho_\phi(r).
   372	\]
   373	
   374	The exact enclosed charge at radius \(r\) is
   375	
   376	\[
   377	Q_\phi(r)=4\pi a r^2e^{-4\phi(r)}(\phi'(r))^3.
   378	\]
   379	
   380	In a source-free annulus it is constant. At a finite seal \(R\), \(\phi(R)=0\) gives \(Q_\phi(R)=4\pi aR^2(\phi'(R))^3\); it does not set \(\phi'(R)\). This result comes from imposing the ansatz on the already varied independent-field equation. It is not claimed equivalent to varying an action after substituting a metric that itself depends on \(\phi\); that separate reduced-variation branch would include extra metric-variation terms.
   381	
   382	**Step Q4 — controlled weak/local expansion, CHOSEN only on the preceding branch.** Let
   383	
   384	\[
   385	\phi=\varepsilon f,\qquad
   386	\delta=|\varepsilon|\sup_V|f|<1,
   387	\]
   388	
   389	with bounded derivatives on the finite region \(V\). Uniformly on \(V\),
   390	
   391	\[
   392	e^{\pm2\phi}=1\pm2\varepsilon f+2\varepsilon^2f^2+R_3^\pm,
   393	\qquad |R_3^\pm|\le {e^{2\delta}(2\delta)^3\over6}.
   394	\]
   395	
   396	The exact radial flux has the expansion
   397	
   398	\[
   399	Q_\phi(r)=4\pi a r^2\varepsilon^3(f')^3
   400	\left[1-4\varepsilon f+O(\varepsilon^2f^2)\right].
   401	\]
   402	
   403	Keeping only the first term is an absolute \(O(\varepsilon^4)\) approximation when \(f,f'\) are uniformly bounded. More explicitly,
   404	
   405	\[
   406	|Q_\phi-Q_{\rm lead}|\le
   407	4\pi|a|r^2|\varepsilon|^3|f'|^3(4\delta e^{4\delta}).
   408	\]
   409	
   410	There is no linear Poisson limit on this branch: the first nonzero scalar operator/flux is cubic in amplitude. That is a counterexample to any claim that weak reciprocal kinematics forces a linear inverse-square gravitational law. The expansion is a local finite-cell readout for \(r\le R\); it neither takes \(R\to\infty\) nor identifies \(Q_\phi\) with mass. A “large-cell” statement would additionally require a chosen local subregion whose size is small compared with a derived cell curvature/variation scale; C0/C1 supply no such numerical scale, so no stronger large-cell estimate is derived.
   411

FILE /stage1/arm_A/cold_output/cas_common_scale.py
     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""Verify common-scale times determinant-one decomposition of a positive diagonal map."""
     3	import sympy as sp
     4	
     5	u, v = sp.symbols("u v", positive=True)
     6	phi = sp.log(v/u) / 2
     7	lhs = sp.diag(u, v)
     8	rhs = sp.sqrt(u*v) * sp.diag(sp.exp(-phi), sp.exp(phi))
     9	print("diag(u,v) - proposed decomposition =")
    10	sp.pprint(sp.simplify(lhs-rhs))
    11	print("determinant of reciprocal factor =", sp.simplify(sp.det(sp.diag(sp.exp(-phi), sp.exp(phi)))))
    12	print("recovered phi =", sp.simplify(phi))

     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""Verify that arbitrary time-sector coefficients leave the supplied static S2 energy unchanged."""
     3	import sympy as sp
     4	
     5	xi, kappa, a2, a4 = sp.symbols("xi kappa a2 a4", real=True)
     6	Dt2, Di2, Fti2, Fij2 = sp.symbols("Dt2 Di2 Fti2 Fij2", nonnegative=True)
     7	# Exact flat-foliation Lagrangian; a2 and a4 are deliberately independent.
     8	L = a2*Dt2/2-xi*Di2/2+a4*Fti2/2-kappa*Fij2/4
     9	print("General flat-foliation L =", L)
    10	L_static = sp.simplify(L.subs({Dt2: 0, Fti2: 0}))
    11	E = xi*Di2/2+kappa*Fij2/4
    12	print("Static L =", L_static)
    13	print("Static L + supplied E =", sp.simplify(L_static+E))
    14	print("d(static L)/d(a2) =", sp.diff(L_static, a2))
    15	print("d(static L)/d(a4) =", sp.diff(L_static, a4))

FILE /stage1/arm_A/cold_output/cas_metric_block.py
     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""Verify reciprocal metric-block identity, inverse, determinant, and spherical lapse-volume cancellation."""
     3	import sympy as sp
     4	
     5	phi, th = sp.symbols("phi theta", real=True)
     6	c, r = sp.symbols("c r", positive=True)
     7	g2 = sp.diag(-sp.exp(-2*phi)*c**2, sp.exp(2*phi))
     8	print("g2 =")
     9	sp.pprint(g2)
    10	print("g2 inverse =")

    13	print("det(g2) =", sp.simplify(g2.det()))
    14	
    15	N = sp.exp(-phi)
    16	hdet = sp.exp(2*phi)*r**4*sp.sin(th)**2
    17	print("N*sqrt(det h) (positive angular chart) =", sp.simplify(N*sp.sqrt(hdet)))
FILE /stage1/arm_A/cold_output/cas_p4_stress.py
     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""Verify trace and static energy density of the four-dimensional X^2 scalar stress tensor."""
     3	import sympy as sp
     4	
     5	a, X = sp.symbols("a X", real=True)
     6	d = sp.symbols("d", integer=True, positive=True)
     7	# T_ab = a X phi_a phi_b - a/4 g_ab X^2, and g^ab phi_a phi_b=X.
     8	trace_d = sp.simplify(a*X**2 - d*a*X**2/4)
     9	print("Trace in d dimensions =", trace_d)
    10	print("Trace in d=4 =", sp.simplify(trace_d.subs(d, 4)))
    11

    13	# In a static orthonormal frame X=p2 and phi_0=0; T_00=-(a/4)g_00 X^2.
    14	T00_static = sp.simplify(a*p2**2/4)
    15	print("Static orthonormal T_00 =", T00_static)
    16	print("Hamiltonian density -L =", sp.simplify(-(-a*p2**2/4)))
    17	print("Difference =", sp.simplify(T00_static-a*p2**2/4))
FILE /stage1/arm_A/cold_output/cas_p4_variation.py
     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""Check the reduced Euler-Lagrange expression and boundary momentum for L=-(a/4)(phi')^4."""
     3	import sympy as sp
     4	
     5	x, a = sp.symbols("x a", real=True)
     6	f = sp.Function("f")(x)
     7	fp = sp.diff(f, x)
     8	L = -a*fp**4/4
     9	momentum = sp.diff(L, fp)
    10	EL = sp.diff(L, f) - sp.diff(momentum, x)
    11	print("L =", L)
    12	print("dL/d(phi') =", momentum)
    13	print("Euler-Lagrange coefficient dL/dphi-d_x(dL/dphi') =")
    14	sp.pprint(sp.factor(EL))
    15	print("Expected a*d_x((phi')^3), difference =", sp.simplify(EL-a*sp.diff(fp**3, x)))
    16	print("Boundary coefficient from integration by parts is dL/d(phi') =", momentum)
FILE /stage1/arm_A/cold_output/cas_radial_flux.py
     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""Verify the exact radial p=4 flux and its small-amplitude expansion in the reciprocal spherical representative."""
     3	import sympy as sp
     4	
     5	r = sp.symbols("r", positive=True)
     6	eps, a = sp.symbols("eps a", real=True)
     7	f = sp.Function("f")(r)
     8	phi = eps*f
     9	phip = sp.diff(phi, r)
    10	# N sqrt(h)=r^2 sin(theta); X=h^rr phi'^2=e^-2phi phi'^2;
    11	# X h^rr phi'=e^-4phi phi'^3.
    12	flux_no_angle = sp.simplify(a*r**2*sp.exp(-4*phi)*phip**3)
    13	print("Exact angle-stripped boundary flux =")
    14	sp.pprint(flux_no_angle)
    15	series_flux = sp.series(flux_no_angle, eps, 0, 5)
    16	print("Expansion through O(eps^4) =")
    17	sp.pprint(series_flux)
    18	leading = a*r**2*eps**3*sp.diff(f, r)**3
    19	print("Exact minus leading, series through O(eps^5) =")
    20	sp.pprint(sp.series(sp.simplify(flux_no_angle-leading), eps, 0, 6))
FILE /stage1/arm_A/cold_output/cas_reciprocity.py
     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""Verify the 2x2 dual-pairing algebra and the reciprocal exponential subgroup."""
     3	import sympy as sp
     4	
     5	u, v, a, b = sp.symbols("u v a b", positive=True)
     6	K = sp.Matrix([[0, 1], [1, 0]])
     7	P = sp.diag(u, v)
     8	print("P.T*K*P =")
     9	sp.pprint(sp.simplify(P.T * K * P))
    10	print("P.T*K*P-K =")
    11	sp.pprint(sp.simplify(P.T * K * P - K))
    12	print("Solutions of uv=1 for v:", sp.solve(sp.Eq(u*v, 1), v))
    13	
    14	Pe = lambda z: sp.diag(sp.exp(-z), sp.exp(z))
    15	print("P(a)P(b)-P(a+b) =")
    16	sp.pprint(sp.simplify(Pe(a) * Pe(b) - Pe(a+b)))
    17	print("P(a).T*K*P(a)-K =")
    18	sp.pprint(sp.simplify(Pe(a).T * K * Pe(a) - K))
FILE /stage1/arm_A/cold_output/cas_s2_static_completion.py
     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""Verify that the chosen Lorentz-covariant S2 completion reduces to minus the supplied static energy."""
     3	import sympy as sp
     4	
     5	xi, kappa = sp.symbols("xi kappa", real=True)
     6	nt2, ns2 = sp.symbols("nt2 ns2", nonnegative=True)
     7	f0i2, fij_sum = sp.symbols("f0i2 fij_sum", nonnegative=True)
     8	# Signature (-,+,+,+): Y=-|dot n|^2+sum_i|d_i n|^2.
     9	Y = -nt2 + ns2
    10	# Ordered-index contraction F_mn F^mn = -2 sum_i F_0i^2 + sum_{i,j}F_ij^2.
    11	F2 = -2*f0i2 + fij_sum
    12	L = -xi*Y/2-kappa*F2/4
    13	print("Four-dimensional Lagrangian in components =")
    14	sp.pprint(sp.expand(L))
    15	L_static = sp.simplify(L.subs({nt2: 0, f0i2: 0}))
    16	E_supplied = xi*ns2/2+kappa*fij_sum/4
    17	print("Static L =", L_static)
    18	print("Supplied static energy density =", E_supplied)
    19	print("L_static + E_supplied =", sp.simplify(L_static+E_supplied))
FILE /stage1/arm_A/cold_output/cas_s2_stress.py
     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""Verify that T_00 of the chosen covariant S2 completion equals the supplied static energy density."""
     3	import sympy as sp
     4	
     5	xi, kappa, ns2, fij_sum = sp.symbols("xi kappa ns2 fij_sum", real=True)
     6	# For static fields in Minkowski: d_0 n=F_0a=0, g_00=-1.
     7	Y = ns2
     8	F2 = fij_sum
     9	T00_sigma = sp.simplify(xi*(0-sp.Rational(1, 2)*(-1)*Y))
    10	T00_F = sp.simplify(kappa*(0-sp.Rational(1, 4)*(-1)*F2))
    11	E = xi*ns2/2+kappa*fij_sum/4
    12	print("T00 sigma =", T00_sigma)
    13	print("T00 quartic =", T00_F)
    14	print("T00 total - supplied energy =", sp.simplify(T00_sigma+T00_F-E))
FILE /stage1/arm_A/cold_output/cas_weak_metric_expansion.py
     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""Verify controlled Taylor expansions of the reciprocal metric factors and their remainders' first omitted order."""
     3	import sympy as sp
     4	
     5	eps, f = sp.symbols("eps f", real=True)
     6	minus = sp.exp(-2*eps*f)
     7	plus = sp.exp(2*eps*f)
     8	print("e^(-2 eps f) through eps^2 =", sp.series(minus, eps, 0, 3))
     9	print("e^(+2 eps f) through eps^2 =", sp.series(plus, eps, 0, 3))
    10	prod_truncated = sp.expand((1-2*eps*f+2*eps**2*f**2)*(1+2*eps*f+2*eps**2*f**2))
    11	print("Product of O(eps^2) truncations through eps^4 =", prod_truncated)
    12	print("Product through retained O(eps^2) =", sp.series(prod_truncated, eps, 0, 3))

# UDT NATIVE ACTION — STAGE-I COLD FOUNDATION PACKET

**Purpose.** This is the complete foundation packet for
`UDT_NATIVE_ACTION_COLD_ARM_DISPATCH.md`. It supplies the owner rulings current through 2026-07-17 and
no information beyond the frozen Stage-I premises. The status labels below are authoritative for
this arm.

## 1. FOUNDING LEDGER

### 1.1 Positional dilation

**FOUNDING**—provenance: owner-stated; not to be re-derived or demoted. Positional dilation is a
founding UDT principle. Under the separately ledgered positional-relativity **POSIT** below, positional comparison
is physical and relational and no position is privileged. The arm's task is to determine what
further action, coupling, boundary, and charge structure follows, not to decide whether positional
dilation exists.

For the currently supplied positive local comparison formalization, the following additional
premises are used. They remain **POSIT**—provenance: positional relativity—unless separately owner-locked:

1. **difference:** comparisons depend on relative positional depth rather than an absolute seat;
2. **composition:** comparisons compose consistently through an intermediate position;
3. **reversal:** reversing a comparison gives its inverse;
4. **nontriviality:** **OBSERVED** in the realized universe, which has nonzero dilation; it is not
   derived from composition. The trivial `phi=0` representation remains mathematically allowed.

### 1.2 Reciprocal-c Identity and UDT Reciprocity

**FOUNDING**—provenance: owner-stated and synchronized by the governing dispatch. `c` and `c^{-1}` are coequal
time-to-length and length-to-time conversion directions, not a one-way speed plus an arithmetic
afterthought.

**FOUNDING**—provenance: synchronized by the governing dispatch. The UDT Reciprocity Principle acts dually on
those conversion directions. For the dimension-matched temporal/radial coframe pair

\[
q=(c\,dt,dr)^T,
\qquad
P(\Delta)=\operatorname{diag}(u(\Delta),v(\Delta)),
\]

one faithful dual-pairing formalization is

\[
K=\begin{pmatrix}0&1\\1&0\end{pmatrix},
\qquad
P^T K P=K.
\]

This gives `uv=1`. The inverse relation is **DERIVED**—provenance: dual-action formulation; the choice
to make positional action contragredient is the stated content of UDT Reciprocity and is not a
theorem of the number `c` alone. Ordinary covariance of the conversion isomorphism would instead
give `u=v`. That alternative is retained only as a logical-independence/circularity check on
Reciprocal-c alone; except for the trivial intersection, it is not an admissible countermodel to the
full synchronized ledger, where dual UDT Reciprocity is founding.

### 1.3 Common-Scale Neutrality

**FOUNDING**—provenance: owner-locked. A simultaneous local common rescaling of temporal and spatial measure
is calibration, not pre-material physics. The pre-scale local geometry is represented by

\[
[g]_{\rm CSN}=\{\Omega(x)^2g:\Omega(x)>0\}.
\]

Any positive diagonal time/radial calibration decomposes as

\[
\operatorname{diag}(u,v)
=\sqrt{uv}\,\operatorname{diag}
\!\left(e^{-\phi},e^{+\phi}\right),
\qquad
\phi=\tfrac12\ln(v/u).
\]

Common-Scale Neutrality declares the first factor calibrational; reciprocal positional depth is the
determinant-one second factor. A native pre-scale law must respect this neutrality, but this
statement does not select locality, derivative order, an action, a boundary term, or material scale.
No primitive local meter, second, mass scale, or gravitational normalization is supplied.
Dimensionful scales—including any normalization later identified as `G`—must emerge from solution,
boundary, global-closure, or matter data.

### 1.4 Owner-ruled founding hierarchy — H2

**H2:** positional dilation remains the owner-stated founding principle. Reciprocal-c Identity, dual
UDT Reciprocity, composition, and the explicit regularity premise put the *reciprocal exponential
functional form* downstream. The local Lorentzian readout, Common-Scale representative, sign/unit of
`phi`, and any slot identification retain their separately ledgered non-derived statuses. No result
may use H2 to demote positional dilation or to claim that Reciprocal-c alone derives the metric.

## 2. DERIVED KINEMATIC CONTENT — AND ITS LIMIT

Under the explicit **POSIT** that relative depth is additive and represented by positive comparisons
that are continuous, measurable, monotone, or otherwise regular enough to exclude pathological
Cauchy solutions,

\[
P(\Delta_1+\Delta_2)=P(\Delta_1)P(\Delta_2)
\]

gives exponentials. Thus the exponential form is **DERIVED / CONDITIONAL**—provenance: composition
plus the regularity premise. With `uv=1` and a **CHOSE** sign/unit convention for `phi`,

\[
P(\phi)=\operatorname{diag}(e^{-\phi},e^{+\phi}).
\]

After choosing a representative of the Common-Scale Neutrality class—for example the `Omega=1`
representative—and using a **POSIT / CHOSE** local Lorentzian quadratic readout in the dimension-matched
coframe, the reciprocal clock/parallel-direction block is

\[
g_{tt}=-e^{-2\phi}c^2,
\qquad
g_{\parallel\parallel}=e^{+2\phi},
\qquad
(-g_{tt}/c^2)g_{\parallel\parallel}=1.
\]

Identifying the reciprocal spatial slot with the `phi`-gradient direction is **CONDITIONAL**—
provenance: slot-identification premise outside a gradient-adapted coframe. A static spherical
specialization makes that direction radial, but staticity, spherical symmetry, diagonality, and an
areal chart are not universal consequences.

The supplied owner locks do not themselves specify the following items. Stage I must determine
whether the **combined** ledger constrains or selects any of them, subject to the countermodel gate:

- the transverse spatial block or full time-live geometry;
- independent dynamical fields or the off-shell variation domain;
- an action, field equation, measure, locality class, or derivative order;
- the finite-cell boundary functional or all admissible boundary variations;
- a matter carrier, four-dimensional matter completion, or coupling weight;
- stress/source normalization, gravitational charge, mass, or an absolute scale.

The displayed representative identity is not invariant under a further common conformal rescaling;
the variation ledger must distinguish the equivalence class from its chosen representative. Any
uniqueness or underdetermination claim must attempt a distinct admissible countermodel under the same
complete frozen ledger.

## 3. FINITE-CELL CANON

UDT's native global setting is a finite mirrored cell, not an asymptotically flat spatial infinity.
Accordingly:

- no spatial-infinity condition or asymptotic charge construction may enter as native input;
- a private wall condition may not be selected merely to hold a configuration together;
- **CANONIZED / BINDING INPUT** for the static `phi` sector—authority and evidence: `CANON.md`,
  C-2026-06-10-2 as clarified by C-2026-07-04-1. The spatial-depth mirror makes `phi` odd at the
  seal, so its value vanishes there while its normal derivative is free;
- that parity statement does not select the action's boundary functional, the boundary data for
  every other field, or a conserved charge;
- a local large-cell or weak-field readout is allowed only after its finite-cell relation and error
  are controlled.

## 4. MATTER-CARRIER STATUS AND CONDITIONAL TEST BRANCH

**WORKING / POSIT**—provenance: historical; presently reopened. The round `S^2` carrier is not fixed
as fundamental UDT content. It may emerge from deeper positional dynamics, remain independently
needed, or be replaced by another carrier. The arm must keep a carrier-agnostic branch alive.

For Stage I, the round-$S^2$ model below is supplied solely as a **CONDITIONAL** test branch. No claim
about the existence, outcome, or validity of any historical calculation is supplied. The model is

\[
\mathbf n:\mathbb R^3\to S^2,
\]

with flat-static functional

\[
E[\mathbf n]
=\int d^3x\left[
\frac{\xi}{2}\,\partial_i\mathbf n\!\cdot\!\partial_i\mathbf n
+\frac{\kappa_4}{4}F_{ij}F_{ij}
\right],
\qquad
F_{ij}=\mathbf n\!\cdot\!(\partial_i\mathbf n\times\partial_j\mathbf n).
\]

Within this conditional branch, selecting the already-ledgered historical **WORKING / POSIT**
round-$S^2$ carrier is a **CHOSE** branch selection; it does not change the carrier's owner status.
The displayed functional is **CONDITIONAL / CHOSE**—provenance: supplied branch-model content. Its
coefficient origin and normalization are **OPEN**: no coefficient is derived, supplied as a primitive
UDT constant, or available as a fit parameter. Stage I must not infer the functional from the
founding kinematics. This branch does not establish carrier uniqueness or emergence.

This three-dimensional functional supplies no unique time-derivative sector, spacetime contractions,
measure dependence, lapse/shift response, stress variation, backreaction rule, or mass identity.
Those are derivation questions or explicit underdeterminations. A result on this conditional branch must
remain conditional and may not eliminate alternative carriers.

## 5. UNIVERSAL UNATTAINABLE SCALE

**WORKING / POSIT:** one universal unattainable `X_max` exists. **OPEN**—provenance: owner lead and
derivation target, not derived—$X_{max}=F(c,M_{\rm total},G)$. Primitive-versus-global origin,
functional form, normalization, and value remain open; dimensional compatibility supplies none of
them. No normalization identified as `G` is yet natively derived or numerically supplied.

- Do not insert a Schwarzschild coefficient or any other imported numerical factor.
- Do not treat dimensional compatibility as a derivation.
- Do not make `X_max` object-specific, locally fitted, or locally varying to close an equation.
- A global-output proposal must avoid circular mass definitions and must supply enough independent
  closure conditions to determine all claimed global constants.

## 6. GLOBAL BOOTSTRAP AND MATTER WINDOW

**WORKING**—provenance: owner-stated principle. The realized universe is a self-consistent global
solution, and matter-bearing global solutions occupy an extremely narrow window in total
density—qualitatively, relative width $\ll 1$—with no center, width, or density value supplied.

- “Total” includes every ledger sector supplied by the eventual theory, not localized visible matter
  alone.
- Total density means that full-ledger mass divided by the proper volume of the complete realized
  spatial cell or slice.
- The full time-live proper volume and physical boundary are not yet derived.
- Recycling among ledger sectors is redistribution and does not change total density when total mass
  and proper volume are fixed.
- No density value or numerical width is supplied. Its center and width must emerge.
- Use proper total density $\rho_{\rm tot}$ and the dimensionless fractional width
  $\Delta\rho/\rho_*\ll1$. No separate nondimensionalizing scale is supplied or to be invented; a
  separately normalized density variable remains **OPEN**.
- No global average may be inserted directly into a local action as a fitted coupling, hard cutoff,
  or effective correction.
- The primary reading constrains complete matter-bearing global solutions. The stronger claim that
  no local carrier solution exists outside the window is a separate **OPEN** fork, not an inference.

This principle is a global selection requirement, not a ready-made local field equation, action, or
carrier.

index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..df1ca2d289ede882f95101be48da9ee05dd81fe8
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cas_common_scale.py
@@ -0,0 +1,12 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Verify common-scale times determinant-one decomposition of a positive diagonal map."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+u, v = sp.symbols("u v", positive=True)
+phi = sp.log(v/u) / 2
+lhs = sp.diag(u, v)
+rhs = sp.sqrt(u*v) * sp.diag(sp.exp(-phi), sp.exp(phi))
+print("diag(u,v) - proposed decomposition =")
+sp.pprint(sp.simplify(lhs-rhs))
+print("determinant of reciprocal factor =", sp.simplify(sp.det(sp.diag(sp.exp(-phi), sp.exp(phi)))))
+print("recovered phi =", sp.simplify(phi))

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Verify reciprocal metric-block identity, inverse, determinant, and spherical lapse-volume cancellation."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+phi, c, r, th = sp.symbols("phi c r theta", real=True, positive=True)
+g2 = sp.diag(-sp.exp(-2*phi)*c**2, sp.exp(2*phi))
+print("g2 =")
+sp.pprint(g2)
+print("g2 inverse =")
+sp.pprint(sp.simplify(g2.inv()))
+print("(-g_tt/c^2)*g_rr =", sp.simplify((-g2[0, 0]/c**2)*g2[1, 1]))
+print("det(g2) =", sp.simplify(g2.det()))
+
+N = sp.exp(-phi)
+hdet = sp.exp(2*phi)*r**4*sp.sin(th)**2
+print("N*sqrt(det h) (positive angular chart) =", sp.simplify(N*sp.sqrt(hdet)))

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Verify trace and static energy density of the four-dimensional X^2 scalar stress tensor."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+a, X = sp.symbols("a X", real=True)
+d = sp.symbols("d", integer=True, positive=True)
+# T_ab = a X phi_a phi_b - a/4 g_ab X^2, and g^ab phi_a phi_b=X.
+trace_d = sp.simplify(a*X**2 - d*a*X**2/4)
+print("Trace in d dimensions =", trace_d)
+print("Trace in d=4 =", sp.simplify(trace_d.subs(d, 4)))
+
+p2 = sp.symbols("p2", nonnegative=True)
+# In a static orthonormal frame X=p2 and phi_0=0; T_00=-(a/4)g_00 X^2.
+T00_static = sp.simplify(a*p2**2/4)
+print("Static orthonormal T_00 =", T00_static)
+print("Hamiltonian density -L =", sp.simplify(-(-a*p2**2/4)))
+print("Difference =", sp.simplify(T00_static-a*p2**2/4))
diff --git a/cas_p4_variation.py b/cas_p4_variation.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..53d45530bb2f19ee773e5e196140181eee0dd157

@@ -0,0 +1,16 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Check the reduced Euler-Lagrange expression and boundary momentum for L=-(a/4)(phi')^4."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+x, a = sp.symbols("x a", real=True)
+f = sp.Function("f")(x)
+fp = sp.diff(f, x)
+L = -a*fp**4/4
+momentum = sp.diff(L, fp)
+EL = sp.diff(L, f) - sp.diff(momentum, x)
+print("L =", L)
+print("dL/d(phi') =", momentum)
+print("Euler-Lagrange coefficient dL/dphi-d_x(dL/dphi') =")
+sp.pprint(sp.factor(EL))
+print("Expected a*d_x((phi')^3), difference =", sp.simplify(EL-a*sp.diff(fp**3, x)))
+print("Boundary coefficient from integration by parts is dL/d(phi') =", momentum)
diff --git a/cas_radial_flux.py b/cas_radial_flux.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..075396aca09dbc0d2bf427b030c1498bf081b26f
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cas_radial_flux.py
@@ -0,0 +1,19 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Verify the exact radial p=4 flux and its small-amplitude expansion in the reciprocal spherical representative."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+r, eps, a = sp.symbols("r eps a", positive=True)
+f = sp.Function("f")(r)
+phi = eps*f
+phip = sp.diff(phi, r)
+# N sqrt(h)=r^2 sin(theta); X=h^rr phi'^2=e^-2phi phi'^2;
+# X h^rr phi'=e^-4phi phi'^3.
+flux_no_angle = sp.simplify(a*r**2*sp.exp(-4*phi)*phip**3)
+print("Exact angle-stripped boundary flux =")
+sp.pprint(flux_no_angle)
+series_flux = sp.series(flux_no_angle, eps, 0, 5)
+print("Expansion through O(eps^4) =")
+sp.pprint(series_flux)
+leading = a*r**2*eps**3*sp.diff(f, r)**3
+print("Exact minus leading, series through O(eps^5) =")
+sp.pprint(sp.series(sp.simplify(flux_no_angle-leading), eps, 0, 6))

@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Verify the 2x2 dual-pairing algebra and the reciprocal exponential subgroup."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+u, v, a, b = sp.symbols("u v a b", positive=True)
+K = sp.Matrix([[0, 1], [1, 0]])
+P = sp.diag(u, v)
+print("P.T*K*P =")
+sp.pprint(sp.simplify(P.T * K * P))
+print("P.T*K*P-K =")
+sp.pprint(sp.simplify(P.T * K * P - K))
+print("Solutions of uv=1 for v:", sp.solve(sp.Eq(u*v, 1), v))
+
+Pe = lambda z: sp.diag(sp.exp(-z), sp.exp(z))
+print("P(a)P(b)-P(a+b) =")
+sp.pprint(sp.simplify(Pe(a) * Pe(b) - Pe(a+b)))
+print("P(a).T*K*P(a)-K =")
+sp.pprint(sp.simplify(Pe(a).T * K * Pe(a) - K))
diff --git a/cas_s2_static_completion.py b/cas_s2_static_completion.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..dfd2a68fd45a3d191a87738b7808cbe37c3b1af6

+++ b/cas_s2_static_completion.py
@@ -0,0 +1,19 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Verify that the chosen Lorentz-covariant S2 completion reduces to minus the supplied static energy."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+xi, kappa = sp.symbols("xi kappa", real=True)
+nt2, ns2 = sp.symbols("nt2 ns2", nonnegative=True)
+f0i2, fij_sum = sp.symbols("f0i2 fij_sum", nonnegative=True)
+# Signature (-,+,+,+): Y=-|dot n|^2+sum_i|d_i n|^2.
+Y = -nt2 + ns2
+# Ordered-index contraction F_mn F^mn = -2 sum_i F_0i^2 + sum_{i,j}F_ij^2.
+F2 = -2*f0i2 + fij_sum
+L = -xi*Y/2-kappa*F2/4
+print("Four-dimensional Lagrangian in components =")
+sp.pprint(sp.expand(L))
+L_static = sp.simplify(L.subs({nt2: 0, f0i2: 0}))
+E_supplied = xi*ns2/2+kappa*fij_sum/4
+print("Static L =", L_static)
+print("Supplied static energy density =", E_supplied)
+print("L_static + E_supplied =", sp.simplify(L_static+E_supplied))

@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Verify that T_00 of the chosen covariant S2 completion equals the supplied static energy density."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+xi, kappa, ns2, fij_sum = sp.symbols("xi kappa ns2 fij_sum", real=True)
+# For static fields in Minkowski: d_0 n=F_0a=0, g_00=-1.
+Y = ns2
+F2 = fij_sum
+T00_sigma = sp.simplify(xi*(0-sp.Rational(1, 2)*(-1)*Y))
+T00_F = sp.simplify(kappa*(0-sp.Rational(1, 4)*(-1)*F2))
+E = xi*ns2/2+kappa*fij_sum/4
+print("T00 sigma =", T00_sigma)
+print("T00 quartic =", T00_F)
+print("T00 total - supplied energy =", sp.simplify(T00_sigma+T00_F-E))

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Verify controlled Taylor expansions of the reciprocal metric factors and their remainders' first omitted order."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+eps, f = sp.symbols("eps f", real=True)
+minus = sp.exp(-2*eps*f)
+plus = sp.exp(2*eps*f)
+print("e^(-2 eps f) through eps^2 =", sp.series(minus, eps, 0, 3))
+print("e^(+2 eps f) through eps^2 =", sp.series(plus, eps, 0, 3))
+prod_truncated = sp.expand((1-2*eps*f+2*eps**2*f**2)*(1+2*eps*f+2*eps**2*f**2))
+print("Product of O(eps^2) truncations through eps^4 =", prod_truncated)
+print("Product through retained O(eps^2) =", sp.series(prod_truncated, eps, 0, 3))

index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..716b8021fc091f397acb369b4609a3d3111a8c80
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cas_reciprocity.py
@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Verify the 2x2 dual-pairing algebra and the reciprocal exponential subgroup."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+u, v, a, b = sp.symbols("u v a b", positive=True)
+K = sp.Matrix([[0, 1], [1, 0]])
+P = sp.diag(u, v)
+print("P.T*K*P =")
+sp.pprint(sp.simplify(P.T * K * P))
+print("P.T*K*P-K =")
+sp.pprint(sp.simplify(P.T * K * P - K))
+print("Solutions of uv=1 for v:", sp.solve(sp.Eq(u*v, 1), v))
+
+Pe = lambda z: sp.diag(sp.exp(-z), sp.exp(z))
+print("P(a)P(b)-P(a+b) =")
+sp.pprint(sp.simplify(Pe(a) * Pe(b) - Pe(a+b)))
+print("P(a).T*K*P(a)-K =")
+sp.pprint(sp.simplify(Pe(a).T * K * Pe(a) - K))
diff --git a/cas_s2_static_completion.py b/cas_s2_static_completion.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..dfd2a68fd45a3d191a87738b7808cbe37c3b1af6
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cas_s2_static_completion.py
@@ -0,0 +1,19 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Verify that the chosen Lorentz-covariant S2 completion reduces to minus the supplied static energy."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+xi, kappa = sp.symbols("xi kappa", real=True)
+nt2, ns2 = sp.symbols("nt2 ns2", nonnegative=True)
+f0i2, fij_sum = sp.symbols("f0i2 fij_sum", nonnegative=True)
+# Signature (-,+,+,+): Y=-|dot n|^2+sum_i|d_i n|^2.
+Y = -nt2 + ns2
+# Ordered-index contraction F_mn F^mn = -2 sum_i F_0i^2 + sum_{i,j}F_ij^2.
+F2 = -2*f0i2 + fij_sum
+L = -xi*Y/2-kappa*F2/4
+print("Four-dimensional Lagrangian in components =")
+sp.pprint(sp.expand(L))
+L_static = sp.simplify(L.subs({nt2: 0, f0i2: 0}))
+E_supplied = xi*ns2/2+kappa*fij_sum/4
+print("Static L =", L_static)
+print("Supplied static energy density =", E_supplied)
+print("L_static + E_supplied =", sp.simplify(L_static+E_supplied))
diff --git a/cas_s2_stress.py b/cas_s2_stress.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..a26f606df212d778103cf01b62f02567b1b937cd
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cas_s2_stress.py
@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Verify that T_00 of the chosen covariant S2 completion equals the supplied static energy density."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+xi, kappa, ns2, fij_sum = sp.symbols("xi kappa ns2 fij_sum", real=True)
+# For static fields in Minkowski: d_0 n=F_0a=0, g_00=-1.
+Y = ns2
+F2 = fij_sum
+T00_sigma = sp.simplify(xi*(0-sp.Rational(1, 2)*(-1)*Y))
+T00_F = sp.simplify(kappa*(0-sp.Rational(1, 4)*(-1)*F2))
+E = xi*ns2/2+kappa*fij_sum/4
+print("T00 sigma =", T00_sigma)
+print("T00 quartic =", T00_F)
+print("T00 total - supplied energy =", sp.simplify(T00_sigma+T00_F-E))
diff --git a/cas_weak_metric_expansion.py b/cas_weak_metric_expansion.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..b465a886d637abbb81b9ee786e8e6e95eab54f89
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cas_weak_metric_expansion.py
@@ -0,0 +1,12 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Verify controlled Taylor expansions of the reciprocal metric factors and their remainders' first omitted order."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+eps, f = sp.symbols("eps f", real=True)
+minus = sp.exp(-2*eps*f)
+plus = sp.exp(2*eps*f)
+print("e^(-2 eps f) through eps^2 =", sp.series(minus, eps, 0, 3))
+print("e^(+2 eps f) through eps^2 =", sp.series(plus, eps, 0, 3))
+prod_truncated = sp.expand((1-2*eps*f+2*eps**2*f**2)*(1+2*eps*f+2*eps**2*f**2))
+print("Product of O(eps^2) truncations through eps^4 =", prod_truncated)
+print("Product through retained O(eps^2) =", sp.series(prod_truncated, eps, 0, 3))

diff --git a/cas_common_scale.py b/cas_common_scale.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..df1ca2d289ede882f95101be48da9ee05dd81fe8
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cas_common_scale.py
@@ -0,0 +1,12 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Verify common-scale times determinant-one decomposition of a positive diagonal map."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+u, v = sp.symbols("u v", positive=True)
+phi = sp.log(v/u) / 2
+lhs = sp.diag(u, v)
+rhs = sp.sqrt(u*v) * sp.diag(sp.exp(-phi), sp.exp(phi))
+print("diag(u,v) - proposed decomposition =")
+sp.pprint(sp.simplify(lhs-rhs))
+print("determinant of reciprocal factor =", sp.simplify(sp.det(sp.diag(sp.exp(-phi), sp.exp(phi)))))
+print("recovered phi =", sp.simplify(phi))
diff --git a/cas_metric_block.py b/cas_metric_block.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..3abfae1170da998e553da3de9bae798b61175ea0
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cas_metric_block.py
@@ -0,0 +1,16 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Verify reciprocal metric-block identity, inverse, determinant, and spherical lapse-volume cancellation."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+phi, c, r, th = sp.symbols("phi c r theta", real=True, positive=True)
+g2 = sp.diag(-sp.exp(-2*phi)*c**2, sp.exp(2*phi))
+print("g2 =")
+sp.pprint(g2)
+print("g2 inverse =")
+sp.pprint(sp.simplify(g2.inv()))
+print("(-g_tt/c^2)*g_rr =", sp.simplify((-g2[0, 0]/c**2)*g2[1, 1]))
+print("det(g2) =", sp.simplify(g2.det()))
+
+N = sp.exp(-phi)
+hdet = sp.exp(2*phi)*r**4*sp.sin(th)**2
+print("N*sqrt(det h) (positive angular chart) =", sp.simplify(N*sp.sqrt(hdet)))
diff --git a/cas_p4_stress.py b/cas_p4_stress.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..188bd82bfea3356d70c0d8d4cdd8349c20f51c73
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cas_p4_stress.py
@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Verify trace and static energy density of the four-dimensional X^2 scalar stress tensor."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+a, X = sp.symbols("a X", real=True)
+d = sp.symbols("d", integer=True, positive=True)
+# T_ab = a X phi_a phi_b - a/4 g_ab X^2, and g^ab phi_a phi_b=X.
+trace_d = sp.simplify(a*X**2 - d*a*X**2/4)
+print("Trace in d dimensions =", trace_d)
+print("Trace in d=4 =", sp.simplify(trace_d.subs(d, 4)))
+
+p2 = sp.symbols("p2", nonnegative=True)
+# In a static orthonormal frame X=p2 and phi_0=0; T_00=-(a/4)g_00 X^2.
+T00_static = sp.simplify(a*p2**2/4)
+print("Static orthonormal T_00 =", T00_static)
+print("Hamiltonian density -L =", sp.simplify(-(-a*p2**2/4)))
+print("Difference =", sp.simplify(T00_static-a*p2**2/4))
diff --git a/cas_p4_variation.py b/cas_p4_variation.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..53d45530bb2f19ee773e5e196140181eee0dd157
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cas_p4_variation.py
@@ -0,0 +1,16 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Check the reduced Euler-Lagrange expression and boundary momentum for L=-(a/4)(phi')^4."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+x, a = sp.symbols("x a", real=True)
+f = sp.Function("f")(x)
+fp = sp.diff(f, x)
+L = -a*fp**4/4
+momentum = sp.diff(L, fp)
+EL = sp.diff(L, f) - sp.diff(momentum, x)
+print("L =", L)
+print("dL/d(phi') =", momentum)
+print("Euler-Lagrange coefficient dL/dphi-d_x(dL/dphi') =")
+sp.pprint(sp.factor(EL))
+print("Expected a*d_x((phi')^3), difference =", sp.simplify(EL-a*sp.diff(fp**3, x)))
+print("Boundary coefficient from integration by parts is dL/d(phi') =", momentum)
diff --git a/cas_radial_flux.py b/cas_radial_flux.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..075396aca09dbc0d2bf427b030c1498bf081b26f
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cas_radial_flux.py
@@ -0,0 +1,19 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Verify the exact radial p=4 flux and its small-amplitude expansion in the reciprocal spherical representative."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+r, eps, a = sp.symbols("r eps a", positive=True)
+f = sp.Function("f")(r)
+phi = eps*f
+phip = sp.diff(phi, r)
+# N sqrt(h)=r^2 sin(theta); X=h^rr phi'^2=e^-2phi phi'^2;
+# X h^rr phi'=e^-4phi phi'^3.
+flux_no_angle = sp.simplify(a*r**2*sp.exp(-4*phi)*phip**3)
+print("Exact angle-stripped boundary flux =")
+sp.pprint(flux_no_angle)
+series_flux = sp.series(flux_no_angle, eps, 0, 5)
+print("Expansion through O(eps^4) =")
+sp.pprint(series_flux)
+leading = a*r**2*eps**3*sp.diff(f, r)**3
+print("Exact minus leading, series through O(eps^5) =")
+sp.pprint(sp.series(sp.simplify(flux_no_angle-leading), eps, 0, 6))

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Verify the 2x2 dual-pairing algebra and the reciprocal exponential subgroup."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+u, v, a, b = sp.symbols("u v a b", positive=True)
+K = sp.Matrix([[0, 1], [1, 0]])
+P = sp.diag(u, v)
+print("P.T*K*P =")
+sp.pprint(sp.simplify(P.T * K * P))
+print("P.T*K*P-K =")
+sp.pprint(sp.simplify(P.T * K * P - K))
+print("Solutions of uv=1 for v:", sp.solve(sp.Eq(u*v, 1), v))
+
+Pe = lambda z: sp.diag(sp.exp(-z), sp.exp(z))
+print("P(a)P(b)-P(a+b) =")
+sp.pprint(sp.simplify(Pe(a) * Pe(b) - Pe(a+b)))
+print("P(a).T*K*P(a)-K =")
+sp.pprint(sp.simplify(Pe(a).T * K * Pe(a) - K))

+xi, kappa = sp.symbols("xi kappa", real=True)
+nt2, ns2 = sp.symbols("nt2 ns2", nonnegative=True)

+Y = -nt2 + ns2

+E_supplied = xi*ns2/2+kappa*fij_sum/4
+print("Static L =", L_static)
+print("Supplied static energy density =", E_supplied)
+print("L_static + E_supplied =", sp.simplify(L_static+E_supplied))

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Verify the 2x2 dual-pairing algebra and the reciprocal exponential subgroup."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+u, v, a, b = sp.symbols("u v a b", positive=True)
+K = sp.Matrix([[0, 1], [1, 0]])
+P = sp.diag(u, v)
+print("P.T*K*P =")
+sp.pprint(sp.simplify(P.T * K * P))
+print("P.T*K*P-K =")
+sp.pprint(sp.simplify(P.T * K * P - K))
+print("Solutions of uv=1 for v:", sp.solve(sp.Eq(u*v, 1), v))
+
+Pe = lambda z: sp.diag(sp.exp(-z), sp.exp(z))
+print("P(a)P(b)-P(a+b) =")

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Verify that the chosen Lorentz-covariant S2 completion reduces to minus the supplied static energy."""
+import sympy as sp
+

+nt2, ns2 = sp.symbols("nt2 ns2", nonnegative=True)

+"""Verify that T_00 of the chosen covariant S2 completion equals the supplied static energy density."""

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Verify controlled Taylor expansions of the reciprocal metric factors and their remainders' first omitted order."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+eps, f = sp.symbols("eps f", real=True)
+minus = sp.exp(-2*eps*f)
+plus = sp.exp(2*eps*f)

+print("e^(+2 eps f) through eps^2 =", sp.series(plus, eps, 0, 3))
+prod_truncated = sp.expand((1-2*eps*f+2*eps**2*f**2)*(1+2*eps*f+2*eps**2*f**2))
+print("Product of O(eps^2) truncations through eps^4 =", prod_truncated)
+print("Product through retained O(eps^2) =", sp.series(prod_truncated, eps, 0, 3))

@@ -0,0 +1,12 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Verify common-scale times determinant-one decomposition of a positive diagonal map."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+u, v = sp.symbols("u v", positive=True)
+phi = sp.log(v/u) / 2
+lhs = sp.diag(u, v)
+rhs = sp.sqrt(u*v) * sp.diag(sp.exp(-phi), sp.exp(phi))
+print("diag(u,v) - proposed decomposition =")
+sp.pprint(sp.simplify(lhs-rhs))
+print("determinant of reciprocal factor =", sp.simplify(sp.det(sp.diag(sp.exp(-phi), sp.exp(phi)))))
+print("recovered phi =", sp.simplify(phi))

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Verify that arbitrary time-sector coefficients leave the supplied static S2 energy unchanged."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+xi, kappa, a2, a4 = sp.symbols("xi kappa a2 a4", real=True)
+Dt2, Di2, Fti2, Fij2 = sp.symbols("Dt2 Di2 Fti2 Fij2", nonnegative=True)
+# Exact flat-foliation Lagrangian; a2 and a4 are deliberately independent.
+L = a2*Dt2/2-xi*Di2/2+a4*Fti2/2-kappa*Fij2/4
+print("General flat-foliation L =", L)
+L_static = sp.simplify(L.subs({Dt2: 0, Fti2: 0}))
+E = xi*Di2/2+kappa*Fij2/4
+print("Static L =", L_static)
+print("Static L + supplied E =", sp.simplify(L_static+E))
+print("d(static L)/d(a2) =", sp.diff(L_static, a2))
+print("d(static L)/d(a4) =", sp.diff(L_static, a4))

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Verify four-dimensional Weyl weights of sqrt(-g) X^2 and of the S2 quadratic/quartic densities."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+Omega = sp.symbols("Omega", positive=True)
+d = sp.symbols("d", integer=True, positive=True)
+# Weight exponents under g_ab -> Omega^2 g_ab.
+sqrtg_w = d
+X_w = -2
+F2_w = -4
+print("weight exponent of sqrt(g)*X^2 in d dimensions =", sp.simplify(sqrtg_w+2*X_w))
+print("in d=4 =", sp.simplify((sqrtg_w+2*X_w).subs(d, 4)))
+print("weight exponent of sqrt(g)*F_mn F^mn in d=4 =", 4+F2_w)
+print("weight exponent of sqrt(g)*g^mn dn.dn in d=4 =", 4-2)
+chi2_w = -2
+print("with a weight -1 compensator chi, quadratic term weight =", 4-2+chi2_w)
+print("explicit invariant factors:", sp.simplify(Omega**4*(Omega**-2)**2), sp.simplify(Omega**4*Omega**-4))

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Verify reciprocal metric-block identity, inverse, determinant, and spherical lapse-volume cancellation."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+phi, c, r, th = sp.symbols("phi c r theta", real=True, positive=True)
+g2 = sp.diag(-sp.exp(-2*phi)*c**2, sp.exp(2*phi))
+print("g2 =")
+sp.pprint(g2)
+print("g2 inverse =")
+sp.pprint(sp.simplify(g2.inv()))
+print("(-g_tt/c^2)*g_rr =", sp.simplify((-g2[0, 0]/c**2)*g2[1, 1]))
+print("det(g2) =", sp.simplify(g2.det()))
+
+N = sp.exp(-phi)
+hdet = sp.exp(2*phi)*r**4*sp.sin(th)**2
+print("N*sqrt(det h) (positive angular chart) =", sp.simplify(N*sp.sqrt(hdet)))

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Verify trace and static energy density of the four-dimensional X^2 scalar stress tensor."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+a, X = sp.symbols("a X", real=True)
+d = sp.symbols("d", integer=True, positive=True)
+# T_ab = a X phi_a phi_b - a/4 g_ab X^2, and g^ab phi_a phi_b=X.
+trace_d = sp.simplify(a*X**2 - d*a*X**2/4)
+print("Trace in d dimensions =", trace_d)
+print("Trace in d=4 =", sp.simplify(trace_d.subs(d, 4)))
+
+p2 = sp.symbols("p2", nonnegative=True)
+# In a static orthonormal frame X=p2 and phi_0=0; T_00=-(a/4)g_00 X^2.
+T00_static = sp.simplify(a*p2**2/4)
+print("Static orthonormal T_00 =", T00_static)
+print("Hamiltonian density -L =", sp.simplify(-(-a*p2**2/4)))
+print("Difference =", sp.simplify(T00_static-a*p2**2/4))

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Check the reduced Euler-Lagrange expression and boundary momentum for L=-(a/4)(phi')^4."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+x, a = sp.symbols("x a", real=True)
+f = sp.Function("f")(x)
+fp = sp.diff(f, x)
+L = -a*fp**4/4
+momentum = sp.diff(L, fp)
+EL = sp.diff(L, f) - sp.diff(momentum, x)
+print("L =", L)
+print("dL/d(phi') =", momentum)
+print("Euler-Lagrange coefficient dL/dphi-d_x(dL/dphi') =")
+sp.pprint(sp.factor(EL))
+print("Expected a*d_x((phi')^3), difference =", sp.simplify(EL-a*sp.diff(fp**3, x)))
+print("Boundary coefficient from integration by parts is dL/d(phi') =", momentum)

index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..075396aca09dbc0d2bf427b030c1498bf081b26f

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Verify the exact radial p=4 flux and its small-amplitude expansion in the reciprocal spherical representative."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+r, eps, a = sp.symbols("r eps a", positive=True)
+f = sp.Function("f")(r)
+phi = eps*f
+phip = sp.diff(phi, r)
+# N sqrt(h)=r^2 sin(theta); X=h^rr phi'^2=e^-2phi phi'^2;
+# X h^rr phi'=e^-4phi phi'^3.
+flux_no_angle = sp.simplify(a*r**2*sp.exp(-4*phi)*phip**3)
+print("Exact angle-stripped boundary flux =")
+sp.pprint(flux_no_angle)
+series_flux = sp.series(flux_no_angle, eps, 0, 5)
+print("Expansion through O(eps^4) =")
+sp.pprint(series_flux)
+leading = a*r**2*eps**3*sp.diff(f, r)**3
+print("Exact minus leading, series through O(eps^5) =")
+sp.pprint(sp.series(sp.simplify(flux_no_angle-leading), eps, 0, 6))

new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..716b8021fc091f397acb369b4609a3d3111a8c80

@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Verify the 2x2 dual-pairing algebra and the reciprocal exponential subgroup."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+u, v, a, b = sp.symbols("u v a b", positive=True)
+K = sp.Matrix([[0, 1], [1, 0]])
+P = sp.diag(u, v)
+print("P.T*K*P =")
+sp.pprint(sp.simplify(P.T * K * P))
+print("P.T*K*P-K =")
+sp.pprint(sp.simplify(P.T * K * P - K))
+print("Solutions of uv=1 for v:", sp.solve(sp.Eq(u*v, 1), v))
+
+Pe = lambda z: sp.diag(sp.exp(-z), sp.exp(z))
+print("P(a)P(b)-P(a+b) =")
+sp.pprint(sp.simplify(Pe(a) * Pe(b) - Pe(a+b)))
+print("P(a).T*K*P(a)-K =")
+sp.pprint(sp.simplify(Pe(a).T * K * Pe(a) - K))

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Verify that arbitrary time-sector coefficients leave the supplied static S2 energy unchanged."""
+import sympy as sp
+
+xi, kappa, a2, a4 = sp.symbols("xi kappa a2 a4", real=True)
+Dt2, Di2, Fti2, Fij2 = sp.symbols("Dt2 Di2 Fti2 Fij2", nonnegative=True)
+# Exact flat-foliation Lagrangian; a2 and a4 are deliberately independent.
+L = a2*Dt2/2-xi*Di2/2+a4*Fti2/2-kappa*Fij2/4
+print("General flat-foliation L =", L)
+L_static = sp.simplify(L.subs({Dt2: 0, Fti2: 0}))
+E = xi*Di2/2+kappa*Fij2/4
+print("Static L =", L_static)
+print("Static L + supplied E =", sp.simplify(L_static+E))
+print("d(static L)/d(a2) =", sp.diff(L_static, a2))
+print("d(static L)/d(a4) =", sp.diff(L_static, a4))
diff --git a/cas_conformal_weights.py b/cas_conformal_weights.py

   109	## D2 - derivation
   110	
   111	### D2.1 Forced reciprocal kinematics
   112	
   113	1. `FORCED-BY(C1 §1.2)` In the supplied dual-pairing formalization,
   114	   \[
   115	   P=\operatorname{diag}(u,v),\qquad
   116	   K=\begin{pmatrix}0&1\\1&0\end{pmatrix},\qquad
   117	   P^T K P = K.
   118	   \]
   119	   Direct multiplication gives
   120	   \[
   121	   P^T K P
   122	   =
   123	   \begin{pmatrix}
   124	   0 & uv\\
   125	   uv & 0
   126	   \end{pmatrix},
   127	   \]
   128	   so dual reciprocity forces
   129	   \[
   130	   uv=1.
   131	   \]
   132	
   133	2. `FORCED-BY(C1 §1.3)` Any positive diagonal map splits algebraically as
   134	   \[
   135	   \operatorname{diag}(u,v)
   136	   =
   137	   \sqrt{uv}\,
   138	   \operatorname{diag}(e^{-phi},e^{+phi}),
   139	   \qquad
   140	   phi=\tfrac12\ln(v/u).
   141	   \]
   142	   Under CSN, the factor `sqrt(uv)` is calibrational. Therefore the determinant-one reciprocal factor is the native non-calibrational content.
   143	
   144	3. `FORCED-BY(C1 §2)` If the relative-depth parameter is additive and the positive comparison law obeys composition with the supplied regularity premise,
   145	   \[
   146	   P(\Delta_1+\Delta_2)=P(\Delta_1)P(\Delta_2),
   147	   \]
   148	   then the scalar diagonal entries are exponential in the additive parameter. Combined with `uv=1`, one may write on the scalar branch
   149	   \[
   150	   P(phi)=\operatorname{diag}(e^{-phi},e^{+phi}),
   151	   \]
   152	   where the sign/unit naming of `phi` is `CHOSE` and the exponential form is `DERIVED / CONDITIONAL`.
   153	
   154	4. `FORCED-BY(C1 §1.4, C1 §2)` H2 fixes the logical order: this reciprocal exponential form is downstream from positional dilation plus reciprocity/composition/regularity; it may not be used to demote positional dilation or claim Reciprocal-c alone derived the metric.
   155	
   156	### D2.2 Forced metric representative identity, and its limit
   157	
   158	5. `CHOSEN(C1 §2)` After choosing:
   159	   - a representative of the CSN class, e.g. `Omega=1`, and
   160	   - a local Lorentzian quadratic readout in the dimension-matched coframe,
   161	   
   162	   the reciprocal block can be written as
   163	   \[
   164	   g_{tt}=-e^{-2phi}c^2,\qquad
   165	   g_{\parallel\parallel}=e^{+2phi},
   166	   \]
   167	   hence
   168	   \[
   169	   (-g_{tt}/c^2)\,g_{\parallel\parallel}=1.
   170	   \]
   171	
   172	6. `FORCED-BY(C1 §2)` The product identity above is representative-dependent, not CSN-invariant. Under `g \mapsto \Omega^2 g`,
   173	   \[
   174	   (-g_{tt}/c^2)\,g_{\parallel\parallel}\mapsto \Omega^4(-g_{tt}/c^2)\,g_{\parallel\parallel}.
   175	   \]
   176	   Therefore the frozen ledger forces a distinction between:
   177	   - the invariant CSN conformal class, and
   178	   - any chosen representative used for explicit equations.
   179	
   180	7. `FORCED-BY(C1 §2)` Nothing in the frozen ledger fixes:
   181	   - the full transverse spatial block,
   182	   - the full time-live metric,
   183	   - a unique set of off-shell fields,
   184	   - a local action, locality class, derivative order, or measure,
   185	   - a finite-cell boundary functional,
   186	   - a matter carrier, 4D matter completion, or source normalization,
   187	   - a native charge/mass law.
   188	
   189	### D2.3 Q1: dynamical completion and countermodel gate
   190	
   191	8. `FORCED-BY(C1 §2, C0 §2.11, C0 §2.12)` A uniqueness claim about the action must survive a distinct admissible countermodel. The frozen ledger itself already says that action, field equation, measure, locality class, and derivative order are not specified by the owner locks alone. So Stage I must test whether the combined ledger nonetheless collapses those freedoms. It does not.
   192	
   193	9. `CHOSEN(F3b)` A representative-based local second-order scalar branch exists once one chooses the scalar `phi` branch and a representative measure:
   194	   \[
   195	   S_{(2)}[\phi]
   196	   =
   197	   \int_{M_4}\mu\,L(\phi,X)
   198	   +
   199	   \int_{\partial M_4} B(\phi),
   200	   \qquad
   201	   X=\nabla_\mu\phi\,\nabla^\mu\phi.
   202	   \]
   203	   Exact nonlinear Euler-Lagrange equation:
   204	   \[
   205	   L_\phi - 2\nabla_\mu\!\left(L_X\nabla^\mu\phi\right)=0.
   206	   \]
   207	   Exact boundary variation term:
   208	   \[
   209	   \delta S_{(2)}\big|_{\partial M_4}
   210	   =
   211	   \int_{\partial M_4}
   212	   \left(2L_X n^\mu\nabla_\mu\phi + B'(\phi)\right)\delta\phi.
   213	   \]
   214	   On the static finite-cell parity branch, `phi|_Sigma=0` and preserving odd parity gives `delta phi|_Sigma=0`; the normal derivative remains free. This makes the parity datum compatible with many bulk/boundary choices, not one unique choice.
   215	
   216	10. `CHOSEN(F3c)` A distinct local higher-derivative branch is also admissible after representative choice:
   217	   \[
   218	   S_{(4)}[\phi]
   219	   =
   220	   \int_{M_4}\mu\,\widetilde L(\phi,X,Y),
   221	   \qquad
   222	   X=\nabla_\mu\phi\,\nabla^\mu\phi,\quad
   223	   Y=\Box\phi.
   224	   \]
   225	   Exact nonlinear field equation:
   226	   \[
   227	   \widetilde L_\phi
   228	   -2\nabla_\mu\!\left(\widetilde L_X\nabla^\mu\phi\right)
   229	   +\Box\!\left(\widetilde L_Y\right)=0.
   230	   \]
   231	   This branch has a different operator order and different boundary data requirements.
   232	
   233	11. `CHOSEN(F3d)` A distinct nonlocal branch also survives the frozen ledger because locality is not derived:
   234	   \[
   235	   S_{\rm nl}[\phi]
   236	   =
   237	   \frac12\int_{M_4}\!\!\mu_x\!\int_{M_4}\!\!\mu_y\,
   238	   K(x,y)\left(\phi(x)-\phi(y)\right)^2
   239	   +
   240	   \int_{\partial M_4}B(\phi).
   241	   \]
   242	   Exact nonlinear integral equation:
   243	   \[
   244	   \int_{M_4}\mu_y\,
   245	   \left(K(x,y)+K(y,x)\right)\left(\phi(x)-\phi(y)\right)=0.
   246	   \]
   247	
   248	12. `COUNTERMODEL RESULT` Because `S_(2)`, `S_(4)`, and `S_nl` are mutually distinct admissible branches once a representative and field branch are chosen, the reciprocal kinematic metric relation does not select a unique action principle, derivative order, locality class, or boundary functional. Q1 is therefore at least `UNDERDETERMINED`, and more strongly yields an `ALLOWED-FAMILY` of dynamical completions.
   249	
   250	13. `FORCED-BY(C0 §2.9, C1 §2)` Before any optional static/spherical/diagonal/areal reduction, the safe Stage-I statement of the off-shell domain is:
   251	   - a finite-cell geometry in the CSN class,
   252	   - reciprocal block data `P` or `phi`,
   253	   - optional additional slot data if the reciprocal direction is not tied to `grad phi`,
   254	   - optional matter fields.
   255	   Any reduced action varied only after imposing static/spherical/areal form is a separate `CONDITIONAL` branch unless equivalence to the unreduced variation is proved. That proof is not forced by the packet and is not supplied here.
   256	
   257	### D2.4 Q2: carrier census and 4D completion
   258	
   259	14. `FORCED-BY(C1 §4)` The carrier is not selected by the frozen foundation. A carrier-agnostic branch must remain live. The supplied round-`S^2` model is conditional only.
   260	
   261	15. `CHOSEN(F5a)` Carrier-agnostic matter branch:
   262	   \[
   263	   S_{\rm m}[\psi_A]
   264	   =
   265	   \int_{M_4}\mu\,L_{\rm m}(\psi_A,\nabla\psi_A,\ldots)
   266	   +
   267	   \int_{\partial M_4}B_{\rm m}.
   268	   \]
   269	   Exact nonlinear equations are the variational equations
   270	   \[
   271	   \frac{\delta S_{\rm m}}{\delta \psi_A}=0.
   272	   \]
   273	   This branch defeats any claim that the packet itself forces a specific carrier.
   274	
   275	16. `CHOSEN(F5b,F6a)` Conditional round-`S^2` covariant completion:
   276	   \[
   277	   \mathbf n:M_4\to S^2,\qquad \mathbf n\cdot\mathbf n=1,
   278	   \]
   279	   \[
   280	   S_{\rm rel}[n,\lambda;g]
   281	   =
   282	   \int d^4x\,\sqrt{|g|}\left[
   283	   \frac{\xi}{2}g^{\mu\nu}\partial_\mu\mathbf n\cdot\partial_\nu\mathbf n
   284	   +
   285	   \frac{\kappa_4}{4}F_{\mu\nu}F^{\mu\nu}
   286	   +
   287	   \lambda(\mathbf n\cdot\mathbf n-1)
   288	   \right],
   289	   \]
   290	   \[
   291	   F_{\mu\nu}
   292	   =
   293	   \mathbf n\cdot(\partial_\mu\mathbf n\times\partial_\nu\mathbf n).
   294	   \]
   295	   Exact nonlinear equations on this branch:
   296	   \[
   297	   \mathbf n\times\left[
   298	   \xi\,\Box_g\mathbf n
   299	   +
   300	   \kappa_4\,\nabla_\mu\!\left(F^{\mu\nu}\partial_\nu\mathbf n\right)
   301	   \right]=0,
   302	   \qquad
   303	   \mathbf n\cdot\mathbf n=1.
   304	   \]
   305	   If one also chooses metric variation, the exact source equation is branch-relative:
   306	   \[
   307	   T_{\mu\nu}^{\rm rel}
   308	   =
   309	   -\frac{2}{\sqrt{|g|}}\frac{\delta S_{\rm rel}}{\delta g^{\mu\nu}}.
   310	   \]
   311	
   312	17. `CHOSEN(F5b,F6b)` A distinct foliation-dependent completion is also admissible because the frozen ledger does not derive Lorentz covariance, time-derivative weights, or the spacetime measure from the static 3D energy:
   313	   \[
   314	   S_{\rm fol}[n,\lambda;N,h]
   315	   =
   316	   \int dt\,d^3x\,N\sqrt{h}\left[
   317	   \frac{\alpha}{2N^2}(D_t\mathbf n)^2
   318	   -\frac{\beta}{2}h^{ij}\partial_i\mathbf n\cdot\partial_j\mathbf n
   319	   -\frac{\gamma}{2N^2}h^{ij}F_{0i}F_{0j}
   320	   -\frac{\delta}{4}h^{ik}h^{jl}F_{ij}F_{kl}
   321	   +\lambda(\mathbf n\cdot\mathbf n-1)
   322	   \right].
   323	   \]
   324	   Exact nonlinear equations are
   325	   \[
   326	   \frac{\delta S_{\rm fol}}{\delta \mathbf n}=0,
   327	   \qquad
   328	   \mathbf n\cdot\mathbf n=1.
   329	   \]
   330	   This branch is dynamically, metrically, and source-wise distinct from `S_rel`.
   331	
   332	18. `COUNTERMODEL RESULT` The pair `(S_rel, S_fol)` defeats uniqueness of:
   333	   - time derivatives,
   334	   - spacetime contractions,
   335	   - measure choice,
   336	   - lapse/shift response,
   337	   - stress variation,
   338	   - backreaction rule.
   339	   Therefore the frozen Stage-I ledger does not select the carrier or a unique 4D completion of the supplied `E2+E4` functional.
   340	
   341	### D2.5 Q3: finite-cell charge, mass, and weak-field readout
   342	
   343	19. `FORCED-BY(C1 §3)` The only exact native finite-cell boundary datum fixed for the static `phi` sector is parity:
   344	   \[
   345	   \phi|_\Sigma=0,
   346	   \qquad
   347	   \partial_n\phi\ \text{free}.
   348	   \]
   349	   This is a boundary condition on the field value, not a derivation of the action boundary functional or a conserved charge.
   350	
   351	20. `FORCED-BY(C1 §2, C1 §3, C0 §2.10)` No unique exact static bulk equation for `phi` is derived by the frozen ledger. The reciprocal metric relation is kinematic; the packet explicitly withholds action, field equation, boundary functional, and mass identity. Therefore no unique native gravitational charge or mass law is forced at Stage I.
   352	
   353	21. `ALLOWED-FAMILY` Once a bulk action branch is chosen, a branch-relative finite-cell flux/charge can be written from the exact boundary variation. For the second-order scalar branch `S_(2)`,
   354	   \[
   355	   q_\Sigma^{(2)}
   356	   :=
   357	   2L_X n^\mu\nabla_\mu\phi + B'(\phi)
   358	   \]
   359	   is the canonical boundary density conjugate to `delta phi`. On the parity-fixed branch, `delta phi|_\Sigma=0`, so the variational principle does not force `q_\Sigma^{(2)}=0`; it only leaves the normal derivative free. This is a valid branch-relative definition, not a derived universal mass law.
   360	
   361	22. `ALLOWED-FAMILY` If a time-live metric action is chosen and a time-translation generator exists, a finite-cell mass can be defined branch-relatively as the corresponding Hamiltonian or quasilocal boundary generator. But because no such metric action or symmetry is derived here, that mass notion remains open.
   362	
   363	23. `FORCED-BY(C0 §1, C0 §2.3, C1 §3)` A controlled weak-field or local-large-cell readout may be performed only after an exact nonlinear branch equation is fixed. Example on the chosen `S_(2)` branch: if
   364	   \[
   365	   \phi = O(\varepsilon),\qquad
   366	   L(\phi,X)\ \text{analytic near}\ \phi=0,\ X=0,
   367	   \]
   368	   then the exact equation
   369	   \[
   370	   L_\phi - 2\nabla_\mu(L_X\nabla^\mu\phi)=0
   371	   \]
   372	   linearizes to
   373	   \[
   374	   \mathcal L_1[\phi]=O(\varepsilon^2)
   375	   \]
   376	   with remainder controlled by the Taylor remainder of `L`. This readout is branch-relative and requires extra analyticity premises not contained in the frozen packet.
   377	
   378	## D3 - algebra checks
   379	
   380	### D3.1 `cas_dual_reciprocity_uv.py`
   381	
   382	- Verifies:
   383	  - direct multiplication of `P^T K P` for diagonal `P`,
   384	  - the exact algebraic reduction to `uv=1`.
   385	- Cannot verify:
   386	  - that the supplied dual-pairing formalization is the only faithful formalization,
   387	  - that dual reciprocity itself is derivable rather than founding,
   388	  - any uniqueness or dynamics claim.
   389	
   390	### D3.2 `cas_csn_decomposition.py`
   391	
   392	- Verifies:
   393	  - the diagonal split into common scale `sqrt(uv)` and reciprocal factor with `phi = (1/2) log(v/u)`,
   394	  - determinant one of the reciprocal factor.
   395	- Cannot verify:
   396	  - Common-Scale Neutrality as a physical premise,
   397	  - whether a native action should be written on the conformal class, a compensator system, or a gauge-fixed representative.
   398	
   399	### D3.3 `cas_exponential_composition.py`
   400	
   401	- Verifies:
   402	  - the exponential reciprocal ansatz satisfies composition,
   403	  - the exponential reciprocal ansatz satisfies reversal/inverse,
   404	  - determinant one of the reciprocal ansatz.
   405	- Cannot verify:
   406	  - the regularity theorem that excludes pathological Cauchy solutions,
   407	  - that the exponential ansatz is unique without the extra regularity premise,
   408	  - the H2 founding-hierarchy ruling.
   409	
   410	### D3.4 `cas_metric_product_and_csn.py`
   411	
   412	- Verifies:
   413	  - the representative-level identity `(-g_tt/c^2) g_parallelparallel = 1` for the chosen reciprocal block,
   414	  - the fact that a further common rescaling multiplies that product by `Omega^4`.
   415	- Cannot verify:
   416	  - whether the Lorentzian quadratic readout is physically selected,
   417	  - the full transverse geometry,
   418	  - any field equation or charge law.
