    20	> variation, and boundary viability remain separate premises.
    21	
    22	## 0. Decisive result
    23	
    24	The Reciprocal-c Identity exposes one further structural choice that was previously hidden.
    25	
    26	Every positive time/radial calibration decomposes uniquely as
    27	
    28	$$
    29	\operatorname{diag}(u,v)
    30	=\underbrace{\sqrt{uv}\,I}_{\text{common scale}}
    31	\underbrace{\operatorname{diag}(e^{-\phi},e^\phi)}_{\text{reciprocal depth}},
    32	$$
    33	
    34	where
    35	
    36	$$
    37	\phi=\frac12\ln\frac vu.
    38	$$
    39	
    40	Reciprocal-c plus UDT Reciprocity fixes the determinant-one depth part. It leaves the common scale
    41	undetermined because simultaneous rescaling of time and length does not change $c$.
    42	
    43	If—and only if—one adds the candidate principle
    44	
    45	> **Common-scale neutrality:** the reciprocal $c/c^{-1}$ identity is the complete local
    46	> time-length standard, so simultaneous local rescaling changes calibration rather than physics,
    47	
    48	then the metric has the local equivalence
    49	
    50	$$
    51	g_{\mu\nu}\sim\Omega(x)^2g_{\mu\nu}.
    52	$$
    53	
    54	Under the further named premises metric-only, four-dimensional locality/covariance, parity-even
    55	bulk, no inserted length, lowest nontrivial derivative order, and unrestricted metric variation,
    56	the bulk action is uniquely
    57	
    58	$$
    59	\boxed{
    60	S_C=\alpha_C\int d^4x\sqrt{-g}\,
    61	C_{\mu\nu\rho\sigma}C^{\mu\nu\rho\sigma}
    62	}
    63	$$
    64	
    65	up to normalization, the Euler topological term, boundary terms, and parity-odd topology.
    66	
    67	This is a **UNIQUE-CONDITIONAL** action branch, not yet an unconditional UDT derivation.
    68	
    69	Its full covariant equation admits WR-L exactly, with $X$ as a solution constant rather than an
    70	action parameter. However WR-L is conformally flat and has zero action density in this branch. The
    71	action admits WR-L but does not select it or assign it local conformal-curvature cost.
    72	
    73	## 1. What is new and what is not
    74	
    75	### New
    76	
    77	The proposed Reciprocal-c foundation naturally separates:
    78	
    79	1. inverse time/radial positional depth, fixed by Reciprocity;
    80	2. common time-length calibration, invisible to the value of $c$.
    81	
    82	This identifies common-scale neutrality as a precise possible bridge from kinematics to a
    83	metric-only action principle.
    84	
    85	### Not yet derived
    86	
    87	The fact that a common scale leaves $c$ unchanged does not logically prove that the scale is
    88	unphysical. Clocks, rulers, matter, or global boundary data could supply an absolute local scale.
    89	Therefore common-scale neutrality remains an **OPEN FOUNDATIONAL CANDIDATE**, not something silently
    90	contained in the arithmetic of $c$.
    91	
    92	## 2. Why the action begins at curvature squared
    93	
    94	Under
    95	
    96	$$
    97	g_{\mu\nu}\mapsto\Omega^2g_{\mu\nu}
    98	$$
    99	
   100	in four dimensions:
   101	
   102	- $\sqrt{-g}$ has conformal weight $+4$;
   103	- the volume term is not invariant;
   104	- $\sqrt{-g}R$ is not locally conformally invariant;
   105	- $C_{\mu\nu\rho\sigma}C^{\mu\nu\rho\sigma}$ has weight $-4$, so its product with
   106	  $\sqrt{-g}$ is invariant.
   107	
   108	At curvature-quadratic order the parity-even basis is
   109	
   110	$$
   111	R^2,
   112	\qquad
   113	R_{\mu\nu}R^{\mu\nu},
   114	\qquad
   115	R_{\mu\nu\rho\sigma}R^{\mu\nu\rho\sigma}.
   116	$$
   117	
   118	The exact identities are
   119	
   120	$$
   121	E_4=R_{\mu\nu\rho\sigma}R^{\mu\nu\rho\sigma}
   122	-4R_{\mu\nu}R^{\mu\nu}+R^2,
   123	$$
   124	
   125	$$
   126	C^2=R_{\mu\nu\rho\sigma}R^{\mu\nu\rho\sigma}
   127	-2R_{\mu\nu}R^{\mu\nu}+\frac13R^2.
   128	$$
   129	
   130	In four dimensions $E_4$ contributes topology/boundary rather than a local bulk equation. Thus
   131	
   132	$$
   133	C^2-E_4
   134	=2R_{\mu\nu}R^{\mu\nu}-\frac23R^2.
   135	$$
   136	
   137	Local common-scale invariance selects $C^2$ inside this basis. Without that new principle,
   138	$R^2$ and $R_{\mu\nu}R^{\mu\nu}$ have independent Euler operators, so metric-only locality remains
   139	nonunique.
   140	
   141	Higher-derivative conformal invariants remain possible if the lowest-derivative premise is dropped.
   142	
   143	## 3. Full covariant equation
   144	
   145	Varying the unrestricted metric gives the trace-free Bach equation
   146	
   147	$$
   148	\boxed{
   149	B_{\mu\nu}=0,
   150	}
   151	$$
   152	
   153	with the convention
   154	
   155	$$
   156	B_{\mu\nu}
   157	=\left(\nabla^\rho\nabla^\sigma+\frac12R^{\rho\sigma}\right)
   158	C_{\mu\rho\nu\sigma}.
   159	$$
   160	
   161	The verifier reconstructs this tensor from the metric rather than importing a precomputed spherical
   162	equation. This is essential because reciprocal reduction before variation can otherwise lose
   163	normal metric equations.
   164	
   165	The equation is fourth differential order. Whether its time-live initial-value problem, finite-cell
   166	boundary terms, energy, and additional modes are acceptable is **OPEN**. Lowest nontrivial
   167	conformal order is not the same as second-order dynamics.
   168	
   169	## 4. Reciprocal static invariant basis
   170	
   171	For
   172	
   173	$$
   174	ds^2=-A(r)c^2dt^2+A(r)^{-1}dr^2+r^2d\Omega^2,
   175	$$
   176	
   177	direct metric calculation gives
   178	
   179	$$
   180	R=-A''-\frac{4A'}r-\frac{2(A-1)}{r^2},
    20	## 0. Result
    21	
    22	\[
    23	\boxed{
    24	\begin{gathered}
    25	\text{The founding positional-dilation postulate determines kinematic metric relations,}\\
    26	\text{but it does not determine what functional UDT extremizes or what dynamical quantity it
    27	conserves.}\\
    28	\text{Even after locality/covariance choices are added, an allowed nonlinear action family
    29	remains.}\\
    30	\text{Carrier covariance fixes a conditional stress tensor, not the geometric operator or
    31	finite-cell charge.}
    32	\end{gathered}}
    33	\]
    34	
    35	The exact status is:
    36	
    37	- **Q1 — dynamical completion:** **UNDERDETERMINED**. Action existence, independent fields,
    38	  variation class, measure, derivative order, and boundary functional are not fixed.
    39	- **Q2 — carrier completion:** **CONDITIONAL MINIMAL branch plus ALLOWED FAMILY**. A clean
    40	  physical-metric completion exists after extra premises, but the flat-static \(E_2+E_4\) energy
    41	  does not select its time sector or source coupling uniquely.
    42	- **Q3 — finite-cell mass:** **OPEN**. The finite-cell parity fixes a boundary value, not a
    43	  boundary generator or charge normalization.
    44	- **\(M_N=2E_4=E_2+E_4\):** reproduced exactly under the existing conditional unrestricted-metric
    45	  action, but not promoted to native UDT.
    46	
    47	This is not a failure to perform the derivation. It is the derivation’s forcedness result.
    48	
    49	## 1. Kinematics defines admissible configurations, not their dynamics
    50	
    51	R1/R2/R3 give, in a gradient-adapted frame,
    52	
    53	\[
    54	g_{tt}=-e^{-2\phi}c^2,\qquad
    55	g_{\parallel\parallel}=e^{2\phi}.
    56	\]
    57	
    58	Write the resulting kinematic restriction schematically as
    59	
    60	\[
    61	C[g,\phi]=0.
    62	\]
    63	
    64	This says which metric components are mutually admissible. It does not supply a map
    65	
    66	\[
    67	\text{history}\longmapsto S[\text{history}]
    68	\]
    69	
    70	or an update law. In particular,
    71	
    72	\[
    73	C[g,\phi]=0
    74	\quad\not\Rightarrow\quad
    75	\delta S[g,\phi]=0
    76	\]
    77	
    78	for any distinguished \(S\).
    79	
    80	The missing information is not merely a coefficient. The foundation leaves open:
    81	
    82	- the transverse metric and shift;
    83	- whether \(\phi\) is an independent scalar, a metric coordinate, or a constrained variable;
    84	- whether the full metric is varied or reciprocity is built in before variation;
    85	- locality, measure, derivative order, time sector, and boundary functional.
    86	
    87	Therefore a full covariant variation cannot be written without first selecting structures the
    88	postulate presently leaves free.
    89	
    90	## 2. Explicit nonlinear counterfamily
    91	
    92	The underdetermination persists even after adding a generous conditional completion. Choose:
    93	
    94	- a local metric or spatial metric \(\mathcal G^{AB}\);
    95	- its invariant measure \(d\mu\);
    96	- \(\phi\) as a scalar on that chosen configuration space;
    97	- an action depending only on first derivatives and respecting \(\phi\mapsto\phi+C\).
    98	
    99	Using the working length \(X\), define
   100	
   101	\[
   102	Y=X^2\mathcal G^{AB}\nabla_A\phi\nabla_B\phi,
   103	\]
   104	
   105	and the exact nonlinear family
   106	
   107	\[
   108	\boxed{
   109	S_F[\phi]
   110	=\int d\mu\,X^{-2}F(Y).
   111	}
   112	\]
   113	
   114	Its full Euler operator is
   115	
   116	\[
   117	\boxed{
   118	\nabla_A\left(2F_Y\nabla^A\phi\right)=0.
   119	}
   120	\]
   121	
   122	Two explicit analytic, reversal-even members are
   123	
   124	\[
   125	F_1(Y)=\frac12Y,
   126	\qquad
   127	F_2(Y)=\frac12Y+\frac{\alpha}{4}Y^2.
   128	\]
   129	
   130	They obey the same R1 shift rule and the same chosen covariance/locality assumptions, but give
   131	
   132	\[
   133	\nabla_A\nabla^A\phi=0,
   134	\]
   135	
   136	versus
   137	
   138	\[
   139	\nabla_A\left[
   140	\left(1+\alpha X^2\nabla_B\phi\nabla^B\phi\right)\nabla^A\phi
   141	\right]=0.
   142	\]
   143	
   144	These are distinct full nonlinear operators for every nonzero \(\alpha\). The second member is not
   145	proposed as UDT physics; it is a countermodel to uniqueness. No founding or derived premise fixes
   146	\(\alpha=0\) or forbids the higher power.
   147	
   148	This establishes:
   149	
   150	\[
   151	\boxed{
   152	\text{R1/R2/R3 plus locality and covariance still do not select a unique bulk functional.}
   153	}
   154	\]
   155	
   156	Other choices—higher derivatives, a bilocal relational action, or no action principle—widen the
   157	space further. They are not needed for the proof.
   158	
   159	## 3. What can be conserved, conditionally
   160	
   161	For the chosen shift-invariant \(S_F\), the conditional bulk current is
   162	
   163	\[
   164	J^A=2F_Y\nabla^A\phi,
   165	\qquad
   166	\nabla_AJ^A=0
   167	\]
   168	
   169	on solutions. Different \(F\) gives a different current constitutive law.
   170	
   171	This does not yet give UDT a native conserved charge:
   172	
   173	1. R1 may be a common-depth gauge redundancy rather than a physical global symmetry.
   174	2. The static finite-cell seal imposes \(\phi=0\). A common shift
   175	   \(\phi\mapsto\phi+C\) does not preserve that fixed boundary representative.
   176	3. The normal derivative at the seal is free, so the boundary flux is not set to zero.
   177	4. For a time-live region,
   178	   \[
   179	   \frac{dQ_\phi}{dt}=-\oint_{\partial\Sigma}J^i\,dS_i,
   180	   \]
    90	
    91	## 2. Q1 — the geometric action is not uniquely forced
    92	
    93	### 2.1 A native, shift-legal counterfamily already exists
    94	
    95	Even after adding the historical constrained-two-player and local-second-order premises, the most
    96	general recorded kinetic/transverse candidate contains a family:
    97	
    98	\[
    99	S_{Z,\mu,W}=\int c\sqrt h\left[
   100	\frac{Z}{2}(\partial_r\phi)^2
   101	+\mu e^\phi K\,\partial_r\phi
   102	+R^{(2)}[h]
   103	+W(\phi)\mathcal K
   104	\right]dt\,dr\,d^2x,
   105	\]
   106	
   107	\[
   108	K_{AB}=\frac{1}{2}e^{-\phi}\partial_r h_{AB},
   109	\qquad
   110	\mathcal K=K_{AB}K^{AB}-K^2.
   111	\]
   112	
   113	Under the global depth shift,
   114	
   115	\[
   116	\phi\mapsto\phi+\lambda,
   117	\qquad
   118	K_{AB}\mapsto e^{-\lambda}K_{AB},
   119	\]
   120	
   121	both $(\partial_r\phi)^2$ and $e^\phi K\partial_r\phi$ have weight zero. In the strict-shift branch,
   122	$W=e^{2\phi}$ also makes $W\mathcal K$ weight zero. Therefore positional-shift bookkeeping fixes
   123	neither $Z$ nor $\mu$. On a round transverse metric $h_{AB}=\rho(r)^2\Omega_{AB}$, the $\phi$ equation
   124	contains
   125	
   126	\[
   127	\partial_r\!\left(Z\rho^2\phi'+2\mu\rho\rho'\right).
   128	\]
   129	
   130	Changing $\mu$ changes the equation for a general transverse profile while preserving the same
   131	shift rule. The CAS check `C1–C2` verifies this exactly. Thus two distinct members, for example
   132	$\mu=0$ and $\mu\ne0$, satisfy the same stated forcing rule. That is already a countermodel to
   133	uniqueness.
   134	
   135	This family is itself **CONDITIONAL** on a radial/transverse split, a local term inventory, a
   136	flat-mismatch criterion, and the treatment of $\phi$ as a longitudinal field. It proves
   137	nonuniqueness inside that chosen class; it does not elevate the class to native law.
   138	
   139	### 2.2 EH is a conditional branch, not a positional-dilation theorem
   140	
   141	If one separately CHOOSES all of the following:
   142	
   143	- an unrestricted four-dimensional metric as the only geometric field;
   144	- local diffeomorphism covariance;
   145	- metric equations of at most second differential order;
   146	- the Lovelock-style minimality premises;
   147	
   148	then the classified bulk action is, up to normalization, cosmological term, topology, and boundary
   149	terms,
   150	
   151	\[
   152	S_g^{\rm cond}
   153	=\frac{1}{2\kappa_g}\int\sqrt{-g}\,(R-2\Lambda)\,d^4x+B_{\partial\mathcal C}.
   154	\]
   155	
   156	This is a valid **CONDITIONAL** route. Positional dilation does not derive the unrestricted variation
   157	space, the derivative-order bound, $\Lambda=0$, $\kappa_g$, or the finite-cell boundary functional.
   158	In particular, native finite-cell canon supplies no spatial infinity from which $\Lambda=0$ or an
   159	asymptotic charge normalization follows. The H3 local-room calculation adds $\Lambda=0$ and $N\to1$
   160	as a scoped comparison choice.
   161	
   162	### 2.3 Restrict-then-vary is not vary-then-restrict
   163	
   164	For the reciprocal static spherical metric
   165	
   166	\[
   167	ds^2=-A(r)c^2dt^2+A(r)^{-1}dr^2+r^2d\Omega^2,
   168	\]
   169	
   170	the exact Ricci scalar is
   171	
   172	\[
   173	R=-A''-\frac{4A'}r+\frac{2(1-A)}{r^2},
   174	\]
   175	
   176	and $\sqrt{-g}/(c\sin\theta)=r^2$. Hence
   177	
   178	\[
   179	r^2R
   180	=\frac{d}{dr}\left[-r^2A'-2rA+2r\right].
   181	\]
   182	
   183	Therefore the EH action restricted to the one-function reciprocal family has
   184	
   185	\[
   186	\frac{\delta S_{EH}^{\rm reduced}}{\delta A}=0
   187	\]
   188	
   189	in the bulk. This is the **V1 tangent-variation result**.
   190	
   191	If instead an unrestricted metric is varied first and reciprocity is imposed on the resulting
   192	equations, the mixed components are
   193	
   194	\[
   195	G^t{}_t=G^r{}_r=\frac{rA'+A-1}{r^2},
   196	\qquad
   197	G^\theta{}_\theta=\frac{1}{2}A''+\frac{A'}{r}.
   198	\]
   199	
   200	The unrestricted vacuum equation gives
   201	
   202	\[
   203	A+rA'=1.
   204	\]
   205	
   206	This is the stronger **V2 equation**. It uses virtual variations normal to the reciprocal
   207	configuration surface and is not the Euler equation of the reduced UDT field space.
   208	
   209	There is a further correction to the historical multiplier discussion. With free warps $\nu,\lambda$
   210	and constraint $\nu+\lambda=0$, a multiplier gives
   211	
   212	\[
   213	E_\nu+\eta=0,
   214	\qquad
   215	E_\lambda+\eta=0,
   216	\qquad
   217	\nu+\lambda=0.
   218	\]
   219	
   220	Eliminating $\eta$ gives only
   221	
   222	\[
   223	E_\nu-E_\lambda=0.
   224	\]
   225	
   226	But on the reciprocal surface the exact EH expressions obey $E_\nu=E_\lambda$ identically. Thus the
   227	multiplier equation is empty there, just like tangent variation. It does **not** imply
   228	$E_\nu=E_\lambda=0$. Setting their common value to zero restores the discarded unrestricted normal
   229	equation by hand. CAS checks `B1–B3` verify this.
   230	
   231	Consequently:
   232	
   233	- “EH reduced on the reciprocal family is empty” is **DERIVED**.
   234	- “EH is therefore excluded or wrong” is **not derived**.
   235	- “A multiplier makes reduced EH equivalent to unrestricted Einstein equations” is **false**.
   236	- “Unrestricted EH, followed by a reciprocal solution restriction” is a distinct, CONDITIONAL
   237	  completion.
   238	
   239	## 3. Q2 — the static carrier energy does not determine a 4D source
   240	
   241	The supplied flat-static functional is
   242	
   243	\[
   244	E[\mathbf n]=\int d^3x\left(\rho_2+\rho_4\right),
   245	\]
   246	
   247	\[
   248	\rho_2=\frac{\xi}{2}\,\partial_i\mathbf n\cdot\partial_i\mathbf n,
   249	\qquad
   250	\rho_4=\frac{\kappa_4}{4}F_{ij}F_{ij},
   251	\qquad
   252	F_{ij}=\mathbf n\cdot(\partial_i\mathbf n\times\partial_j\mathbf n).
   253	\]
   254	
   255	It fixes an energy on one reference slice. It does not state how time derivatives enter, which metric
   256	contracts them, how the lapse or shift enters, or what is held fixed during metric variation.
   257	
   258	### 3.1 Conditional minimal physical-metric completion
   259	
   260	The H3 branch CHOOSES

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Arm-C algebra for total derivatives, boundary momentum, and EH reduction.
+
+Certifies that a total derivative preserves the bulk Euler equation while
+shifting boundary momentum, and that reciprocal-spherical EH is a radial total
+derivative. It cannot select boundary data, corner terms, reference subtraction,
+charge normalization, topology, or a differentiable gravitational action.
+"""
+import sympy as sp
+
+checks=[]
+def check(label, condition):
+    ok=bool(condition); checks.append(ok); print(("PASS " if ok else "FAIL ")+label)
+
+r=sp.symbols("r", positive=True)
+lam=sp.symbols("lambda", real=True)
+f=sp.Function("f")(r)
+fp=sp.diff(f,r)
+L0=fp**2/2
+F=lam*f
+L1=L0+sp.diff(F,r)
+def EL(L):
+    return sp.simplify(sp.diff(L,f)-sp.diff(sp.diff(L,fp),r))
+check("total derivative leaves bulk Euler equation unchanged", sp.simplify(EL(L1)-EL(L0))==0)
+p0,p1=sp.diff(L0,fp),sp.diff(L1,fp)
+check("total derivative shifts boundary momentum", sp.simplify(p1-p0-lam)==0)
+check("Dirichlet parity does not set normal derivative", p0 != 0)
+
+# Reciprocal spherical EH identity for ds2=-A dt2+A^-1 dr2+r2dOmega2.
+A=sp.Function("A")(r)
+R=-sp.diff(A,r,2)-4*sp.diff(A,r)/r+2*(1-A)/r**2
+primitive=-r**2*sp.diff(A,r)-2*r*A+2*r
+check("r^2 R is an exact radial total derivative", sp.simplify(r**2*R-sp.diff(primitive,r))==0)
+
+# Reference subtraction and overall normalization remain independent algebraically.
+q,qref,k=sp.symbols("q q_ref k", real=True)
+Q=k*(q-qref)
+check("reference and normalization are independent charge choices", sp.diff(Q,qref)==-k and sp.diff(Q,k)==q-qref)
+
+print("BOUNDARY_MOMENTUM_BEFORE",p0)
+print("BOUNDARY_MOMENTUM_AFTER",p1)
+print("EH_PRIMITIVE",primitive)
+print("LIMIT: bulk algebra does not select a finite-cell primitive, subtraction, or normalized charge")
+print(("PASS" if all(checks) else "FAIL")+f" SUMMARY {sum(checks)}/{len(checks)} checks")
+

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Arm-C exact carrier convention, covariance, and source-channel audit.
+
+Certifies encoded signs, ordered antisymmetric-index factors, static reduction,
+Weyl exponents, source identities, generic direct-weight chain rules, and a
+same-static-energy time-sector kernel.  It cannot select the carrier, action,
+time variable, representative, weights, measure, variation domain, boundary
+data, or a physical source/mass law.
+"""
+import sympy as sp
+
+checks = []
+def check(label, condition):
+    ok = bool(condition)
+    checks.append(ok)
+    print(("PASS " if ok else "FAIL ") + label)
+
+N, sqh = sp.symbols("N sqrt_h", positive=True)
+xi, k4 = sp.symbols("xi kappa4", real=True)
+Dt2, Xs, E0, B = sp.symbols("Dt2 Xs E0 B", real=True)
+a2, a4 = sp.symbols("a2 a4", real=True)
+
+# (-,+,+,+), zero shift. B and E0 use ordered contractions.
+F2 = B - 2*E0/N**2
+Lcov = sp.expand(-N*sqh*(xi*(-Dt2/N**2 + Xs)/2 + k4*F2/4))
+Lstatic = sp.simplify(Lcov.subs({Dt2: 0, E0: 0}))
+expected = -N*sqh*(xi*Xs/2 + k4*B/4)
+check("corrected covariant action reduces to minus positive static E2+E4", sp.simplify(Lstatic-expected) == 0)
+
+# Matching foliation form has positive electric quartic term.
+Lfol = N*sqh*(a2*Dt2/(2*N**2) - xi*Xs/2 + a4*E0/(2*N**2) - k4*B/4)
+check("covariant/foliation mapping is a2=xi and a4=kappa4", sp.simplify(Lfol.subs({a2:xi,a4:k4})-Lcov) == 0)
+check("time coefficients disappear from static restriction", sp.diff(Lfol.subs({Dt2:0,E0:0}), a2) == 0 and sp.diff(Lfol.subs({Dt2:0,E0:0}), a4) == 0)
+
+# Independent spatial components and ordered-index convention.
+xpar, xp1, xp2 = sp.symbols("xpar xp1 xp2", nonnegative=True)
+f12, f13, f23 = sp.symbols("f12 f13 f23", real=True)
+X = xpar + xp1 + xp2
+Y = 2*(f12**2 + f13**2 + f23**2)
+rho2, rho4 = xi*X/2, k4*Y/4
+S2, S4 = -rho2, rho4
+check("unrestricted metric identity rho+S=2rho4", sp.simplify(rho2+rho4+S2+S4-2*rho4) == 0)
+p2par = xi*(xpar-X/2)
+p4par = k4*(f12**2+f13**2-Y/4)
+rho2par = xi*xpar/2
+rho4par = k4*(f12**2+f13**2)/2
+constrained = sp.simplify(rho2+rho4+p2par+p4par)
+check("reciprocal tangent identity is directional", sp.simplify(constrained-2*(rho2par+rho4par)) == 0)
+check("directional and traced sources are generically unequal", sp.simplify(constrained-2*rho4) != 0)
+
+# Direct position-dependent weights are additional functional freedom.
+phi = sp.symbols("phi", real=True)
+u2p,u2t,u4p,u4t = sp.symbols("u2p u2t u4p u4t")
+b2p,b2t,b4p,b4t = sp.symbols("b2p b2t b4p b4t")
+weighted = (sp.exp((b2p-2)*phi)*u2p + sp.exp(b2t*phi)*u2t
+            + sp.exp((b4p-2)*phi)*u4p + sp.exp(b4t*phi)*u4t)
+source0 = sp.expand(sp.diff(weighted, phi).subs(phi, 0))
+expected_source = (b2p-2)*u2p+b2t*u2t+(b4p-2)*u4p+b4t*u4t
+check("chosen carrier weights change every source coefficient", sp.simplify(source0-expected_source) == 0)
+
+# Weyl weights in d=4 for weight-zero n.
+check("carrier L2 and L4 have different CSN weights", (4-2)==2 and (4-4)==0)
+
+print("F_MUNU_FMUNU", F2)
+print("SOURCE_TRACED", sp.simplify(2*rho4))
+print("SOURCE_DIRECTIONAL", constrained)
+print("SOURCE_DIFFERENCE", sp.factor(constrained-2*rho4))
+print("LIMIT: encoded algebra does not select covariance, weights, variation domain, or mass")
+print(("PASS" if all(checks) else "FAIL") + f" SUMMARY {sum(checks)}/{len(checks)} checks")
+

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Arm-C independent three-part mass/virial accounting.
+
+Certifies the conditional stress/source identity and bookkeeping implications.
+It cannot prove EH, minimal coupling, a finite-cell mass generator, a local
+surface theorem, the reported numerical gaps, exact criticality, or a
+controlled asymptotic/domain limit.
+"""
+import sympy as sp
+
+checks=[]
+def check(label, condition):
+    ok=bool(condition); checks.append(ok); print(("PASS " if ok else "FAIL ")+label)
+
+E2,E4,B,W=sp.symbols("E2 E4 B_boundary W_res", real=True)
+M0=2*E4
+Ecar=E2+E4
+defect=sp.expand(M0-(Ecar+B+W))
+check("finite-cell virial implies M0=Ecarrier+B+W", sp.simplify(defect.subs(E4,E2+B+W))==0)
+check("exact criticality W=0 still leaves boundary term", sp.simplify((M0-Ecar).subs({E4:E2+B,W:0})-B)==0)
+check("separate B=W=0 closure gives M0=Ecarrier", sp.simplify((M0-Ecar).subs(E4,E2+B+W).subs({B:0,W:0}))==0)
+
+# Exact source identity for the chosen minimal 4D L2+L4 completion.
+rho2,rho4=sp.symbols("rho2 rho4", real=True)
+S2,S4=-rho2,rho4
+check("minimal carrier gives rho+S=2rho4", sp.simplify((rho2+rho4)+(S2+S4)-2*rho4)==0)
+
+# A nonzero reported relative virial gap algebraically forbids E2=E4.
+gap=sp.Rational(-27,1000)
+check("nonzero -2.7 percent gap forbids finite-box E2=E4", gap != 0)
+
+print("FINITE_CELL_DEFECT",defect)
+print("REPORTED_GAP_RATIONAL",gap)
+print("LIMIT: identities are conditional bookkeeping; surface theorem, numerics, and limits remain open")
+print(("PASS" if all(checks) else "FAIL")+f" SUMMARY {sum(checks)}/{len(checks)} checks")
+

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Arm-C exact algebra for the strongest conditional unique-action attempt.
+
+Certifies only Weyl-weight bookkeeping, curvature-basis identities, and the
+finite linear algebra of the C^2/Euler decomposition.  It cannot certify that
+UDT forces metric-only fields, locality, covariance, polynomiality, parity,
+four-derivative minimality, unrestricted variation, a boundary completion, or
+existence/stability of a nontrivial finite-cell solution.
+"""
+import sympy as sp
+
+checks = []
+
+def check(label, condition):
+    ok = bool(condition)
+    checks.append(ok)
+    print(("PASS " if ok else "FAIL ") + label)
+
+d = sp.symbols("d", integer=True, positive=True)
+# Constant-Weyl exponents are necessary checks. Local Weyl transformations add
+# derivative terms to R and therefore make EH even less invariant.
+weights = {
+    "volume": d,
+    "EH": d - 2,
+    "C2": d - 4,
+    "scalar_X2_weight0_phi": d - 4,
+    "S2_quadratic_weight0_n": d - 2,
+    "S2_F2_weight0_n": d - 4,
+}
+at4 = {k: sp.simplify(v.subs(d, 4)) for k, v in weights.items()}
+check("four-dimensional C^2 density has Weyl exponent zero", at4["C2"] == 0)
+check("four-dimensional scalar X^2 density has Weyl exponent zero", at4["scalar_X2_weight0_phi"] == 0)
+check("EH density is not scale neutral", at4["EH"] == 2)
+check("constant-coefficient S2 quadratic density is not scale neutral", at4["S2_quadratic_weight0_n"] == 2)
+check("S2 F^2 density is scale neutral", at4["S2_F2_weight0_n"] == 0)
+
+# Coefficient vectors use the basis (Riemann^2, Ricci^2, R^2).
+C2 = sp.Matrix([1, -2, sp.Rational(1, 3)])
+Euler = sp.Matrix([1, -4, 1])
+p, q = sp.symbols("p q")
+combo = p*C2 + q*Euler
+check("C^2 identity coefficients", C2 == sp.Matrix([1, -2, sp.Rational(1, 3)]))
+check("Euler identity coefficients", Euler == sp.Matrix([1, -4, 1]))
+check("C^2 and Euler are linearly independent", sp.Matrix.hstack(C2, Euler).rank() == 2)
+
+# Modulo the Euler topological vector, the admitted local parity-even
+# curvature-squared Weyl-invariant bulk direction has one coefficient p.
+alpha, beta, gamma = sp.symbols("alpha beta gamma")
+sol = sp.solve(
+    [sp.Eq(alpha, combo[0]), sp.Eq(beta, combo[1]), sp.Eq(gamma, combo[2])],
+    [alpha, beta, gamma], dict=True
+)
+check("conditional curvature-square family is p*C^2+q*Euler", len(sol) == 1)
+check("Euler quotient leaves one bulk normalization p", sp.Matrix.hstack(C2).rank() == 1)
+
+# Lovelock inventory: volume and EH fail even constant local-scale neutrality;
+# Euler is topological in four dimensions and supplies no local metric equation.
+check("EH/Lovelock and exact pre-scale CSN are in tension", at4["volume"] != 0 and at4["EH"] != 0)
+
+print("WEIGHTS_D4", at4)
+print("CURVATURE_COMBINATION", list(combo))
+print("LIMIT: conditional classification only; premises, boundary law, and solutions are not certified")
+print(("PASS" if all(checks) else "FAIL") + f" SUMMARY {sum(checks)}/{len(checks)} checks")
+

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Arm-C finite-dimensional model of vary/restrict/multiplier alternatives.
+
+The calculation certifies the chain-rule loss of the normal Euler equation
+under a hard reciprocal constraint. It is an algebraic analogue, not a proof
+of any continuum action, constraint qualification, gauge fixing, or boundary
+legality.
+"""
+import sympy as sp
+
+checks=[]
+def check(label, condition):
+    ok=bool(condition); checks.append(ok); print(("PASS " if ok else "FAIL ")+label)
+
+x,y,a,b,c,lam = sp.symbols("x y a b c lambda", real=True)
+L = a*x**2/2 + b*y**2/2 + c*x*y
+Ex, Ey = sp.diff(L,x), sp.diff(L,y)
+
+# Reciprocal hard constraint C=x+y=0, hence y=-x and tangent (dx,dy)=(1,-1).
+Lr = sp.expand(L.subs(y,-x))
+Er = sp.diff(Lr,x)
+check("restrict-then-vary gives tangent projection Ex-Ey", sp.simplify(Er-(Ex-Ey).subs(y,-x))==0)
+normal = sp.simplify((Ex+Ey).subs(y,-x))
+check("a separate normal equation remains", normal != 0)
+
+# Multiplier imposes C=0 but gives only equality Ex=Ey after lambda elimination.
+ELx = Ex+lam
+ELy = Ey+lam
+check("multiplier elimination gives Ex-Ey=0", sp.simplify(ELx-ELy-(Ex-Ey))==0)
+
+# Concrete witness: L=x+y vanishes after restriction, yet both full Euler
+# components are nonzero. Tangent variation and multiplier stationarity allow it.
+Lw=x+y
+Ewx,Ewy=sp.diff(Lw,x),sp.diff(Lw,y)
+check("tangent-stationary witness is not full-stationary", sp.diff(Lw.subs(y,-x),x)==0 and (Ewx!=0 or Ewy!=0))
+check("multiplier can solve witness equations", sp.solve([Ewx+lam,Ewy+lam],[lam], dict=True)==[{lam:-1}])
+
+# Least-squares normal equations are another theory: stationary points of E^2
+# need not be zeros without rank assumptions.
+z=sp.symbols("z", real=True)
+E=sp.Matrix([z**2+1])
+normal_eq=sp.diff((E.dot(E))/2,z)
+check("normal equation can vanish while original equation does not", normal_eq.subs(z,0)==0 and E[0].subs(z,0)!=0)
+
+print("FULL_EULER", Ex, Ey)
+print("TANGENT_EULER", Er)
+print("NORMAL_EULER_ON_CONSTRAINT", normal)
+print("LIMIT: finite chain-rule model only; continuum/gauge/boundary equivalence is not certified")
+print(("PASS" if all(checks) else "FAIL")+f" SUMMARY {sum(checks)}/{len(checks)} checks")
+

+++ b/cas_armc_boundary_charge.py
@@ -0,0 +1,45 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Arm-C algebra for total derivatives, boundary momentum, and EH reduction.
+
+Certifies that a total derivative preserves the bulk Euler equation while
+shifting boundary momentum, and that reciprocal-spherical EH is a radial total
+derivative. It cannot select boundary data, corner terms, reference subtraction,
+charge normalization, topology, or a differentiable gravitational action.
+"""
+import sympy as sp
+
+checks=[]
+def check(label, condition):
+    ok=bool(condition); checks.append(ok); print(("PASS " if ok else "FAIL ")+label)
+
+r=sp.symbols("r", positive=True)
+lam=sp.symbols("lambda", real=True)
+f=sp.Function("f")(r)
+fp=sp.diff(f,r)
+L0=fp**2/2
+F=lam*f
+L1=L0+sp.diff(F,r)
+def EL(L):
+    return sp.simplify(sp.diff(L,f)-sp.diff(sp.diff(L,fp),r))
+check("total derivative leaves bulk Euler equation unchanged", sp.simplify(EL(L1)-EL(L0))==0)
+p0,p1=sp.diff(L0,fp),sp.diff(L1,fp)
+check("total derivative shifts boundary momentum", sp.simplify(p1-p0-lam)==0)
+check("Dirichlet parity does not set normal derivative", p0 != 0)
+
+# Reciprocal spherical EH identity for ds2=-A dt2+A^-1 dr2+r2dOmega2.
+A=sp.Function("A")(r)
+R=-sp.diff(A,r,2)-4*sp.diff(A,r)/r+2*(1-A)/r**2
+primitive=-r**2*sp.diff(A,r)-2*r*A+2*r
+check("r^2 R is an exact radial total derivative", sp.simplify(r**2*R-sp.diff(primitive,r))==0)
+
+# Reference subtraction and overall normalization remain independent algebraically.
+q,qref,k=sp.symbols("q q_ref k", real=True)
+Q=k*(q-qref)
+check("reference and normalization are independent charge choices", sp.diff(Q,qref)==-k and sp.diff(Q,k)==q-qref)
+
+print("BOUNDARY_MOMENTUM_BEFORE",p0)
+print("BOUNDARY_MOMENTUM_AFTER",p1)
+print("EH_PRIMITIVE",primitive)
+print("LIMIT: bulk algebra does not select a finite-cell primitive, subtraction, or normalized charge")
+print(("PASS" if all(checks) else "FAIL")+f" SUMMARY {sum(checks)}/{len(checks)} checks")
+
diff --git a/cas_armc_carrier_covariance.py b/cas_armc_carrier_covariance.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..cbc7817dca4313e0b3e7759638d41c913dfc6fcb
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cas_armc_carrier_covariance.py
@@ -0,0 +1,70 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Arm-C exact carrier convention, covariance, and source-channel audit.
+
+Certifies encoded signs, ordered antisymmetric-index factors, static reduction,
+Weyl exponents, source identities, generic direct-weight chain rules, and a
+same-static-energy time-sector kernel.  It cannot select the carrier, action,
+time variable, representative, weights, measure, variation domain, boundary
+data, or a physical source/mass law.
+"""
+import sympy as sp
+
+checks = []
+def check(label, condition):
+    ok = bool(condition)
+    checks.append(ok)
+    print(("PASS " if ok else "FAIL ") + label)
+
+N, sqh = sp.symbols("N sqrt_h", positive=True)
+xi, k4 = sp.symbols("xi kappa4", real=True)
+Dt2, Xs, E0, B = sp.symbols("Dt2 Xs E0 B", real=True)
+a2, a4 = sp.symbols("a2 a4", real=True)
+
+# (-,+,+,+), zero shift. B and E0 use ordered contractions.
+F2 = B - 2*E0/N**2
+Lcov = sp.expand(-N*sqh*(xi*(-Dt2/N**2 + Xs)/2 + k4*F2/4))
+Lstatic = sp.simplify(Lcov.subs({Dt2: 0, E0: 0}))
+expected = -N*sqh*(xi*Xs/2 + k4*B/4)
+check("corrected covariant action reduces to minus positive static E2+E4", sp.simplify(Lstatic-expected) == 0)
+
+# Matching foliation form has positive electric quartic term.
+Lfol = N*sqh*(a2*Dt2/(2*N**2) - xi*Xs/2 + a4*E0/(2*N**2) - k4*B/4)
+check("covariant/foliation mapping is a2=xi and a4=kappa4", sp.simplify(Lfol.subs({a2:xi,a4:k4})-Lcov) == 0)
+check("time coefficients disappear from static restriction", sp.diff(Lfol.subs({Dt2:0,E0:0}), a2) == 0 and sp.diff(Lfol.subs({Dt2:0,E0:0}), a4) == 0)
+
+# Independent spatial components and ordered-index convention.
+xpar, xp1, xp2 = sp.symbols("xpar xp1 xp2", nonnegative=True)
+f12, f13, f23 = sp.symbols("f12 f13 f23", real=True)
+X = xpar + xp1 + xp2
+Y = 2*(f12**2 + f13**2 + f23**2)
+rho2, rho4 = xi*X/2, k4*Y/4
+S2, S4 = -rho2, rho4
+check("unrestricted metric identity rho+S=2rho4", sp.simplify(rho2+rho4+S2+S4-2*rho4) == 0)
+p2par = xi*(xpar-X/2)
+p4par = k4*(f12**2+f13**2-Y/4)
+rho2par = xi*xpar/2
+rho4par = k4*(f12**2+f13**2)/2
+constrained = sp.simplify(rho2+rho4+p2par+p4par)
+check("reciprocal tangent identity is directional", sp.simplify(constrained-2*(rho2par+rho4par)) == 0)
+check("directional and traced sources are generically unequal", sp.simplify(constrained-2*rho4) != 0)
+
+# Direct position-dependent weights are additional functional freedom.
+phi = sp.symbols("phi", real=True)
+u2p,u2t,u4p,u4t = sp.symbols("u2p u2t u4p u4t")
+b2p,b2t,b4p,b4t = sp.symbols("b2p b2t b4p b4t")
+weighted = (sp.exp((b2p-2)*phi)*u2p + sp.exp(b2t*phi)*u2t
+            + sp.exp((b4p-2)*phi)*u4p + sp.exp(b4t*phi)*u4t)
+source0 = sp.expand(sp.diff(weighted, phi).subs(phi, 0))
+expected_source = (b2p-2)*u2p+b2t*u2t+(b4p-2)*u4p+b4t*u4t
+check("chosen carrier weights change every source coefficient", sp.simplify(source0-expected_source) == 0)
+
+# Weyl weights in d=4 for weight-zero n.
+check("carrier L2 and L4 have different CSN weights", (4-2)==2 and (4-4)==0)
+
+print("F_MUNU_FMUNU", F2)
+print("SOURCE_TRACED", sp.simplify(2*rho4))
+print("SOURCE_DIRECTIONAL", constrained)
+print("SOURCE_DIFFERENCE", sp.factor(constrained-2*rho4))
+print("LIMIT: encoded algebra does not select covariance, weights, variation domain, or mass")
+print(("PASS" if all(checks) else "FAIL") + f" SUMMARY {sum(checks)}/{len(checks)} checks")
+
diff --git a/cas_armc_mass_virial.py b/cas_armc_mass_virial.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..ca2715c86653c741c72b5f9a1f219af971a19464
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cas_armc_mass_virial.py
@@ -0,0 +1,36 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Arm-C independent three-part mass/virial accounting.
+
+Certifies the conditional stress/source identity and bookkeeping implications.
+It cannot prove EH, minimal coupling, a finite-cell mass generator, a local
+surface theorem, the reported numerical gaps, exact criticality, or a
+controlled asymptotic/domain limit.
+"""
+import sympy as sp
+
+checks=[]
+def check(label, condition):
+    ok=bool(condition); checks.append(ok); print(("PASS " if ok else "FAIL ")+label)
+
+E2,E4,B,W=sp.symbols("E2 E4 B_boundary W_res", real=True)
+M0=2*E4
+Ecar=E2+E4
+defect=sp.expand(M0-(Ecar+B+W))
+check("finite-cell virial implies M0=Ecarrier+B+W", sp.simplify(defect.subs(E4,E2+B+W))==0)
+check("exact criticality W=0 still leaves boundary term", sp.simplify((M0-Ecar).subs({E4:E2+B,W:0})-B)==0)
+check("separate B=W=0 closure gives M0=Ecarrier", sp.simplify((M0-Ecar).subs(E4,E2+B+W).subs({B:0,W:0}))==0)
+
+# Exact source identity for the chosen minimal 4D L2+L4 completion.
+rho2,rho4=sp.symbols("rho2 rho4", real=True)
+S2,S4=-rho2,rho4
+check("minimal carrier gives rho+S=2rho4", sp.simplify((rho2+rho4)+(S2+S4)-2*rho4)==0)
+
+# A nonzero reported relative virial gap algebraically forbids E2=E4.
+gap=sp.Rational(-27,1000)
+check("nonzero -2.7 percent gap forbids finite-box E2=E4", gap != 0)
+
+print("FINITE_CELL_DEFECT",defect)
+print("REPORTED_GAP_RATIONAL",gap)
+print("LIMIT: identities are conditional bookkeeping; surface theorem, numerics, and limits remain open")
+print(("PASS" if all(checks) else "FAIL")+f" SUMMARY {sum(checks)}/{len(checks)} checks")
+
diff --git a/cas_armc_unique_action_weights.py b/cas_armc_unique_action_weights.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..22423096e149a9ae1bb92cc916691c057ac40200
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cas_armc_unique_action_weights.py
@@ -0,0 +1,64 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Arm-C exact algebra for the strongest conditional unique-action attempt.
+
+Certifies only Weyl-weight bookkeeping, curvature-basis identities, and the
+finite linear algebra of the C^2/Euler decomposition.  It cannot certify that
+UDT forces metric-only fields, locality, covariance, polynomiality, parity,
+four-derivative minimality, unrestricted variation, a boundary completion, or
+existence/stability of a nontrivial finite-cell solution.
+"""
+import sympy as sp
+
+checks = []
+
+def check(label, condition):
+    ok = bool(condition)
+    checks.append(ok)
+    print(("PASS " if ok else "FAIL ") + label)
+
+d = sp.symbols("d", integer=True, positive=True)
+# Constant-Weyl exponents are necessary checks. Local Weyl transformations add
+# derivative terms to R and therefore make EH even less invariant.
+weights = {
+    "volume": d,
+    "EH": d - 2,
+    "C2": d - 4,
+    "scalar_X2_weight0_phi": d - 4,
+    "S2_quadratic_weight0_n": d - 2,
+    "S2_F2_weight0_n": d - 4,
+}
+at4 = {k: sp.simplify(v.subs(d, 4)) for k, v in weights.items()}
+check("four-dimensional C^2 density has Weyl exponent zero", at4["C2"] == 0)
+check("four-dimensional scalar X^2 density has Weyl exponent zero", at4["scalar_X2_weight0_phi"] == 0)
+check("EH density is not scale neutral", at4["EH"] == 2)
+check("constant-coefficient S2 quadratic density is not scale neutral", at4["S2_quadratic_weight0_n"] == 2)
+check("S2 F^2 density is scale neutral", at4["S2_F2_weight0_n"] == 0)
+
+# Coefficient vectors use the basis (Riemann^2, Ricci^2, R^2).
+C2 = sp.Matrix([1, -2, sp.Rational(1, 3)])
+Euler = sp.Matrix([1, -4, 1])
+p, q = sp.symbols("p q")
+combo = p*C2 + q*Euler
+check("C^2 identity coefficients", C2 == sp.Matrix([1, -2, sp.Rational(1, 3)]))
+check("Euler identity coefficients", Euler == sp.Matrix([1, -4, 1]))
+check("C^2 and Euler are linearly independent", sp.Matrix.hstack(C2, Euler).rank() == 2)
+
+# Modulo the Euler topological vector, the admitted local parity-even
+# curvature-squared Weyl-invariant bulk direction has one coefficient p.
+alpha, beta, gamma = sp.symbols("alpha beta gamma")
+sol = sp.solve(
+    [sp.Eq(alpha, combo[0]), sp.Eq(beta, combo[1]), sp.Eq(gamma, combo[2])],
+    [alpha, beta, gamma], dict=True
+)
+check("conditional curvature-square family is p*C^2+q*Euler", len(sol) == 1)
+check("Euler quotient leaves one bulk normalization p", sp.Matrix.hstack(C2).rank() == 1)
+
+# Lovelock inventory: volume and EH fail even constant local-scale neutrality;
+# Euler is topological in four dimensions and supplies no local metric equation.
+check("EH/Lovelock and exact pre-scale CSN are in tension", at4["volume"] != 0 and at4["EH"] != 0)
+
+print("WEIGHTS_D4", at4)
+print("CURVATURE_COMBINATION", list(combo))
+print("LIMIT: conditional classification only; premises, boundary law, and solutions are not certified")
+print(("PASS" if all(checks) else "FAIL") + f" SUMMARY {sum(checks)}/{len(checks)} checks")
+
diff --git a/cas_armc_variation_domain.py b/cas_armc_variation_domain.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..68e93b859414a32cc4daebf5e27553bfcae0aefa
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cas_armc_variation_domain.py
@@ -0,0 +1,50 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Arm-C finite-dimensional model of vary/restrict/multiplier alternatives.
+
+The calculation certifies the chain-rule loss of the normal Euler equation
+under a hard reciprocal constraint. It is an algebraic analogue, not a proof
+of any continuum action, constraint qualification, gauge fixing, or boundary
+legality.
+"""
+import sympy as sp
+
+checks=[]
+def check(label, condition):
+    ok=bool(condition); checks.append(ok); print(("PASS " if ok else "FAIL ")+label)
+
+x,y,a,b,c,lam = sp.symbols("x y a b c lambda", real=True)
+L = a*x**2/2 + b*y**2/2 + c*x*y
+Ex, Ey = sp.diff(L,x), sp.diff(L,y)
+
+# Reciprocal hard constraint C=x+y=0, hence y=-x and tangent (dx,dy)=(1,-1).
+Lr = sp.expand(L.subs(y,-x))
+Er = sp.diff(Lr,x)
+check("restrict-then-vary gives tangent projection Ex-Ey", sp.simplify(Er-(Ex-Ey).subs(y,-x))==0)
+normal = sp.simplify((Ex+Ey).subs(y,-x))
+check("a separate normal equation remains", normal != 0)
+
+# Multiplier imposes C=0 but gives only equality Ex=Ey after lambda elimination.
+ELx = Ex+lam
+ELy = Ey+lam
+check("multiplier elimination gives Ex-Ey=0", sp.simplify(ELx-ELy-(Ex-Ey))==0)
+
+# Concrete witness: L=x+y vanishes after restriction, yet both full Euler
+# components are nonzero. Tangent variation and multiplier stationarity allow it.
+Lw=x+y
+Ewx,Ewy=sp.diff(Lw,x),sp.diff(Lw,y)
+check("tangent-stationary witness is not full-stationary", sp.diff(Lw.subs(y,-x),x)==0 and (Ewx!=0 or Ewy!=0))
+check("multiplier can solve witness equations", sp.solve([Ewx+lam,Ewy+lam],[lam], dict=True)==[{lam:-1}])
+
+# Least-squares normal equations are another theory: stationary points of E^2
+# need not be zeros without rank assumptions.
+z=sp.symbols("z", real=True)
+E=sp.Matrix([z**2+1])
+normal_eq=sp.diff((E.dot(E))/2,z)
+check("normal equation can vanish while original equation does not", normal_eq.subs(z,0)==0 and E[0].subs(z,0)!=0)
+
+print("FULL_EULER", Ex, Ey)
+print("TANGENT_EULER", Er)
+print("NORMAL_EULER_ON_CONSTRAINT", normal)
+print("LIMIT: finite chain-rule model only; continuum/gauge/boundary equivalence is not certified")
+print(("PASS" if all(checks) else "FAIL")+f" SUMMARY {sum(checks)}/{len(checks)} checks")
+

exec
/bin/bash -lc 'ls -l /return/cas_armc_*.py
python3 -m py_compile /return/cas_armc_*.py
for f in /return/cas_armc_*.py; do out="${f%.py}_out.txt"; python3 "$f" > "$out"; echo "$(basename "$f") exit=$?"; done
for f in /return/cas_armc_*_out.txt; do echo "FILE $(basename "$f")"; sed -n '"'1,80p' \""'$f"; done' in /return

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Arm-C independent three-part mass/virial accounting.
+

+import sympy as sp

+checks=[]
+def check(label, condition):
+    ok=bool(condition); checks.append(ok); print(("PASS " if ok else "FAIL ")+label)
+
+E2,E4,B,W=sp.symbols("E2 E4 B_boundary W_res", real=True)
+M0=2*E4
+Ecar=E2+E4
+defect=sp.expand(M0-(Ecar+B+W))
+check("finite-cell virial implies M0=Ecarrier+B+W", sp.simplify(defect.subs(E4,E2+B+W))==0)
+check("exact criticality W=0 still leaves boundary term", sp.simplify((M0-Ecar).subs({E4:E2+B,W:0})-B)==0)
+check("separate B=W=0 closure gives M0=Ecarrier", sp.simplify((M0-Ecar).subs(E4,E2+B+W).subs({B:0,W:0}))==0)
+
+# Exact source identity for the chosen minimal 4D L2+L4 completion.
+rho2,rho4=sp.symbols("rho2 rho4", real=True)
+S2,S4=-rho2,rho4
+check("minimal carrier gives rho+S=2rho4", sp.simplify((rho2+rho4)+(S2+S4)-2*rho4)==0)
+
+# A nonzero reported relative virial gap algebraically forbids E2=E4.
+gap=sp.Rational(-27,1000)
+check("nonzero -2.7 percent gap forbids finite-box E2=E4", gap != 0)
+
+print("FINITE_CELL_DEFECT",defect)
+print("REPORTED_GAP_RATIONAL",gap)
+print("LIMIT: identities are conditional bookkeeping; surface theorem, numerics, and limits remain open")
+print(("PASS" if all(checks) else "FAIL")+f" SUMMARY {sum(checks)}/{len(checks)} checks")
+

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Arm-C exact algebra for the strongest conditional unique-action attempt.
+
+Certifies only Weyl-weight bookkeeping, curvature-basis identities, and the
+finite linear algebra of the C^2/Euler decomposition.  It cannot certify that
+UDT forces metric-only fields, locality, covariance, polynomiality, parity,
+four-derivative minimality, unrestricted variation, a boundary completion, or
+existence/stability of a nontrivial finite-cell solution.
+"""
+import sympy as sp
+
+checks = []
+
+def check(label, condition):
+    ok = bool(condition)
+    checks.append(ok)
+    print(("PASS " if ok else "FAIL ") + label)
+
+d = sp.symbols("d", integer=True, positive=True)
+# Constant-Weyl exponents are necessary checks. Local Weyl transformations add
+# derivative terms to R and therefore make EH even less invariant.
+weights = {
+    "volume": d,
+    "EH": d - 2,
+    "C2": d - 4,
+    "scalar_X2_weight0_phi": d - 4,
+    "S2_quadratic_weight0_n": d - 2,
+    "S2_F2_weight0_n": d - 4,
+}
+at4 = {k: sp.simplify(v.subs(d, 4)) for k, v in weights.items()}
+check("four-dimensional C^2 density has Weyl exponent zero", at4["C2"] == 0)
+check("four-dimensional scalar X^2 density has Weyl exponent zero", at4["scalar_X2_weight0_phi"] == 0)
+check("EH density is not scale neutral", at4["EH"] == 2)
+check("constant-coefficient S2 quadratic density is not scale neutral", at4["S2_quadratic_weight0_n"] == 2)
+check("S2 F^2 density is scale neutral", at4["S2_F2_weight0_n"] == 0)
+
+# Coefficient vectors use the basis (Riemann^2, Ricci^2, R^2).
+C2 = sp.Matrix([1, -2, sp.Rational(1, 3)])
+Euler = sp.Matrix([1, -4, 1])
+p, q = sp.symbols("p q")
+combo = p*C2 + q*Euler
+check("C^2 identity coefficients", C2 == sp.Matrix([1, -2, sp.Rational(1, 3)]))
+check("Euler identity coefficients", Euler == sp.Matrix([1, -4, 1]))
+check("C^2 and Euler are linearly independent", sp.Matrix.hstack(C2, Euler).rank() == 2)
+
+# Modulo the Euler topological vector, the admitted local parity-even
+# curvature-squared Weyl-invariant bulk direction has one coefficient p.
+alpha, beta, gamma = sp.symbols("alpha beta gamma")
+sol = sp.solve(
+    [sp.Eq(alpha, combo[0]), sp.Eq(beta, combo[1]), sp.Eq(gamma, combo[2])],
+    [alpha, beta, gamma], dict=True
+)
+check("conditional curvature-square family is p*C^2+q*Euler", len(sol) == 1)
+check("Euler quotient leaves one bulk normalization p", sp.Matrix.hstack(C2).rank() == 1)
+
+# Lovelock inventory: volume and EH fail even constant local-scale neutrality;
+# Euler is topological in four dimensions and supplies no local metric equation.
+check("EH/Lovelock and exact pre-scale CSN are in tension", at4["volume"] != 0 and at4["EH"] != 0)
+
+print("WEIGHTS_D4", at4)
+print("CURVATURE_COMBINATION", list(combo))
+print("LIMIT: conditional classification only; premises, boundary law, and solutions are not certified")
+print(("PASS" if all(checks) else "FAIL") + f" SUMMARY {sum(checks)}/{len(checks)} checks")
+

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Arm-C finite-dimensional model of vary/restrict/multiplier alternatives.
+
+The calculation certifies the chain-rule loss of the normal Euler equation
+under a hard reciprocal constraint. It is an algebraic analogue, not a proof
+of any continuum action, constraint qualification, gauge fixing, or boundary
+legality.
+"""
+import sympy as sp
+
+checks=[]
+def check(label, condition):
+    ok=bool(condition); checks.append(ok); print(("PASS " if ok else "FAIL ")+label)
+
+x,y,a,b,c,lam = sp.symbols("x y a b c lambda", real=True)
+L = a*x**2/2 + b*y**2/2 + c*x*y
+Ex, Ey = sp.diff(L,x), sp.diff(L,y)
+
+# Reciprocal hard constraint C=x+y=0, hence y=-x and tangent (dx,dy)=(1,-1).
+Lr = sp.expand(L.subs(y,-x))
+Er = sp.diff(Lr,x)
+check("restrict-then-vary gives tangent projection Ex-Ey", sp.simplify(Er-(Ex-Ey).subs(y,-x))==0)
+normal = sp.simplify((Ex+Ey).subs(y,-x))
+check("a separate normal equation remains", normal != 0)
+
+# Multiplier imposes C=0 but gives only equality Ex=Ey after lambda elimination.
+ELx = Ex+lam
+ELy = Ey+lam
+check("multiplier elimination gives Ex-Ey=0", sp.simplify(ELx-ELy-(Ex-Ey))==0)
+
+# Concrete witness: L=x+y vanishes after restriction, yet both full Euler
+# components are nonzero. Tangent variation and multiplier stationarity allow it.
+Lw=x+y
+Ewx,Ewy=sp.diff(Lw,x),sp.diff(Lw,y)
+check("tangent-stationary witness is not full-stationary", sp.diff(Lw.subs(y,-x),x)==0 and (Ewx!=0 or Ewy!=0))
+check("multiplier can solve witness equations", sp.solve([Ewx+lam,Ewy+lam],[lam], dict=True)==[{lam:-1}])
+
+# Least-squares normal equations are another theory: stationary points of E^2
+# need not be zeros without rank assumptions.
+z=sp.symbols("z", real=True)
+E=sp.Matrix([z**2+1])
+normal_eq=sp.diff((E.dot(E))/2,z)
+check("normal equation can vanish while original equation does not", normal_eq.subs(z,0)==0 and E[0].subs(z,0)!=0)
+
+print("FULL_EULER", Ex, Ey)
+print("TANGENT_EULER", Er)
+print("NORMAL_EULER_ON_CONSTRAINT", normal)
+print("LIMIT: finite chain-rule model only; continuum/gauge/boundary equivalence is not certified")
+print(("PASS" if all(checks) else "FAIL")+f" SUMMARY {sum(checks)}/{len(checks)} checks")
+

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Arm-C algebra for total derivatives, boundary momentum, and EH reduction.
+
+Certifies that a total derivative preserves the bulk Euler equation while
+shifting boundary momentum, and that reciprocal-spherical EH is a radial total
+derivative. It cannot select boundary data, corner terms, reference subtraction,
+charge normalization, topology, or a differentiable gravitational action.
+"""
+import sympy as sp
+
+checks=[]
+def check(label, condition):
+    ok=bool(condition); checks.append(ok); print(("PASS " if ok else "FAIL ")+label)
+
+r=sp.symbols("r", positive=True)
+lam=sp.symbols("lambda", real=True)
+f=sp.Function("f")(r)
+fp=sp.diff(f,r)
+L0=fp**2/2
+F=lam*f
+L1=L0+sp.diff(F,r)
+def EL(L):
+    return sp.simplify(sp.diff(L,f)-sp.diff(sp.diff(L,fp),r))
+check("total derivative leaves bulk Euler equation unchanged", sp.simplify(EL(L1)-EL(L0))==0)
+p0,p1=sp.diff(L0,fp),sp.diff(L1,fp)
+check("total derivative shifts boundary momentum", sp.simplify(p1-p0-lam)==0)
+check("Dirichlet parity does not set normal derivative", p0 != 0)
+
+# Reciprocal spherical EH identity for ds2=-A dt2+A^-1 dr2+r2dOmega2.
+A=sp.Function("A")(r)
+R=-sp.diff(A,r,2)-4*sp.diff(A,r)/r+2*(1-A)/r**2
+primitive=-r**2*sp.diff(A,r)-2*r*A+2*r
+check("r^2 R is an exact radial total derivative", sp.simplify(r**2*R-sp.diff(primitive,r))==0)
+
+# Reference subtraction and overall normalization remain independent algebraically.
+q,qref,k=sp.symbols("q q_ref k", real=True)
+Q=k*(q-qref)
+check("reference and normalization are independent charge choices", sp.diff(Q,qref)==-k and sp.diff(Q,k)==q-qref)
+
+print("BOUNDARY_MOMENTUM_BEFORE",p0)
+print("BOUNDARY_MOMENTUM_AFTER",p1)
+print("EH_PRIMITIVE",primitive)
+print("LIMIT: bulk algebra does not select a finite-cell primitive, subtraction, or normalized charge")
+print(("PASS" if all(checks) else "FAIL")+f" SUMMARY {sum(checks)}/{len(checks)} checks")
+

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Arm-C exact carrier convention, covariance, and source-channel audit.
+
+Certifies encoded signs, ordered antisymmetric-index factors, static reduction,
+Weyl exponents, source identities, generic direct-weight chain rules, and a
+same-static-energy time-sector kernel.  It cannot select the carrier, action,
+time variable, representative, weights, measure, variation domain, boundary
+data, or a physical source/mass law.
+"""
+import sympy as sp
+
+checks = []
+def check(label, condition):
+    ok = bool(condition)
+    checks.append(ok)
+    print(("PASS " if ok else "FAIL ") + label)
+
+N, sqh = sp.symbols("N sqrt_h", positive=True)
+xi, k4 = sp.symbols("xi kappa4", real=True)
+Dt2, Xs, E0, B = sp.symbols("Dt2 Xs E0 B", real=True)
+a2, a4 = sp.symbols("a2 a4", real=True)
+
+# (-,+,+,+), zero shift. B and E0 use ordered contractions.
+F2 = B - 2*E0/N**2
+Lcov = sp.expand(-N*sqh*(xi*(-Dt2/N**2 + Xs)/2 + k4*F2/4))
+Lstatic = sp.simplify(Lcov.subs({Dt2: 0, E0: 0}))
+expected = -N*sqh*(xi*Xs/2 + k4*B/4)
+check("corrected covariant action reduces to minus positive static E2+E4", sp.simplify(Lstatic-expected) == 0)
+
+# Matching foliation form has positive electric quartic term.
+Lfol = N*sqh*(a2*Dt2/(2*N**2) - xi*Xs/2 + a4*E0/(2*N**2) - k4*B/4)
+check("covariant/foliation mapping is a2=xi and a4=kappa4", sp.simplify(Lfol.subs({a2:xi,a4:k4})-Lcov) == 0)
+check("time coefficients disappear from static restriction", sp.diff(Lfol.subs({Dt2:0,E0:0}), a2) == 0 and sp.diff(Lfol.subs({Dt2:0,E0:0}), a4) == 0)
+
+# Independent spatial components and ordered-index convention.
+xpar, xp1, xp2 = sp.symbols("xpar xp1 xp2", nonnegative=True)
+f12, f13, f23 = sp.symbols("f12 f13 f23", real=True)
+X = xpar + xp1 + xp2
+Y = 2*(f12**2 + f13**2 + f23**2)
+rho2, rho4 = xi*X/2, k4*Y/4
+S2, S4 = -rho2, rho4
+check("unrestricted metric identity rho+S=2rho4", sp.simplify(rho2+rho4+S2+S4-2*rho4) == 0)
+p2par = xi*(xpar-X/2)
+p4par = k4*(f12**2+f13**2-Y/4)
+rho2par = xi*xpar/2
+rho4par = k4*(f12**2+f13**2)/2
+constrained = sp.simplify(rho2+rho4+p2par+p4par)
+check("reciprocal tangent identity is directional", sp.simplify(constrained-2*(rho2par+rho4par)) == 0)
+check("directional and traced sources are generically unequal", sp.simplify(constrained-2*rho4) != 0)
+
+# Direct position-dependent weights are additional functional freedom.
+phi = sp.symbols("phi", real=True)
+u2p,u2t,u4p,u4t = sp.symbols("u2p u2t u4p u4t")
+b2p,b2t,b4p,b4t = sp.symbols("b2p b2t b4p b4t")
+weighted = (sp.exp((b2p-2)*phi)*u2p + sp.exp(b2t*phi)*u2t
+            + sp.exp((b4p-2)*phi)*u4p + sp.exp(b4t*phi)*u4t)
+source0 = sp.expand(sp.diff(weighted, phi).subs(phi, 0))
+expected_source = (b2p-2)*u2p+b2t*u2t+(b4p-2)*u4p+b4t*u4t
+check("chosen carrier weights change every source coefficient", sp.simplify(source0-expected_source) == 0)
+
+# Weyl weights in d=4 for weight-zero n.
+check("carrier L2 and L4 have different CSN weights", (4-2)==2 and (4-4)==0)
+
+print("F_MUNU_FMUNU", F2)
+print("SOURCE_TRACED", sp.simplify(2*rho4))
+print("SOURCE_DIRECTIONAL", constrained)
+print("SOURCE_DIFFERENCE", sp.factor(constrained-2*rho4))
+print("LIMIT: encoded algebra does not select covariance, weights, variation domain, or mass")
+print(("PASS" if all(checks) else "FAIL") + f" SUMMARY {sum(checks)}/{len(checks)} checks")
+

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Arm-C exact algebra for the strongest conditional unique-action attempt.
+
+Certifies only Weyl-weight bookkeeping, curvature-basis identities, and the
+finite linear algebra of the C^2/Euler decomposition.  It cannot certify that
+UDT forces metric-only fields, locality, covariance, polynomiality, parity,
+four-derivative minimality, unrestricted variation, a boundary completion, or
+existence/stability of a nontrivial finite-cell solution.
+"""
+import sympy as sp
+
+checks = []
+
+def check(label, condition):
+    ok = bool(condition)
+    checks.append(ok)
+    print(("PASS " if ok else "FAIL ") + label)
+
+d = sp.symbols("d", integer=True, positive=True)
+# Constant-Weyl exponents are necessary checks. Local Weyl transformations add
+# derivative terms to R and therefore make EH even less invariant.
+weights = {
+    "volume": d,
+    "EH": d - 2,
+    "C2": d - 4,
+    "scalar_X2_weight0_phi": d - 4,
+    "S2_quadratic_weight0_n": d - 2,
+    "S2_F2_weight0_n": d - 4,
+}
+at4 = {k: sp.simplify(v.subs(d, 4)) for k, v in weights.items()}
+check("four-dimensional C^2 density has Weyl exponent zero", at4["C2"] == 0)
+check("four-dimensional scalar X^2 density has Weyl exponent zero", at4["scalar_X2_weight0_phi"] == 0)
+check("EH density is not scale neutral", at4["EH"] == 2)
+check("constant-coefficient S2 quadratic density is not scale neutral", at4["S2_quadratic_weight0_n"] == 2)
+check("S2 F^2 density is scale neutral", at4["S2_F2_weight0_n"] == 0)
+
+# Coefficient vectors use the basis (Riemann^2, Ricci^2, R^2).
+C2 = sp.Matrix([1, -2, sp.Rational(1, 3)])
+Euler = sp.Matrix([1, -4, 1])
+p, q = sp.symbols("p q")
+combo = p*C2 + q*Euler
+check("C^2 identity coefficients", C2 == sp.Matrix([1, -2, sp.Rational(1, 3)]))
+check("Euler identity coefficients", Euler == sp.Matrix([1, -4, 1]))
+check("C^2 and Euler are linearly independent", sp.Matrix.hstack(C2, Euler).rank() == 2)
+
+# Modulo the Euler topological vector, the admitted local parity-even
+# curvature-squared Weyl-invariant bulk direction has one coefficient p.
+alpha, beta, gamma = sp.symbols("alpha beta gamma")
+sol = sp.solve(
+    [sp.Eq(alpha, combo[0]), sp.Eq(beta, combo[1]), sp.Eq(gamma, combo[2])],
+    [alpha, beta, gamma], dict=True
+)
+check("conditional curvature-square family is p*C^2+q*Euler", len(sol) == 1)
+check("Euler quotient leaves one bulk normalization p", sp.Matrix.hstack(C2).rank() == 1)
+
+# Lovelock inventory: volume and EH fail even constant local-scale neutrality;
+# Euler is topological in four dimensions and supplies no local metric equation.
+check("EH/Lovelock and exact pre-scale CSN are in tension", at4["volume"] != 0 and at4["EH"] != 0)
+
+print("WEIGHTS_D4", at4)
+print("CURVATURE_COMBINATION", list(combo))
+print("LIMIT: conditional classification only; premises, boundary law, and solutions are not certified")
+print(("PASS" if all(checks) else "FAIL") + f" SUMMARY {sum(checks)}/{len(checks)} checks")
+
diff --git a/cas_armc_variation_domain.py b/cas_armc_variation_domain.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..68e93b859414a32cc4daebf5e27553bfcae0aefa
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cas_armc_variation_domain.py
@@ -0,0 +1,50 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Arm-C finite-dimensional model of vary/restrict/multiplier alternatives.
+
+The calculation certifies the chain-rule loss of the normal Euler equation
+under a hard reciprocal constraint. It is an algebraic analogue, not a proof
+of any continuum action, constraint qualification, gauge fixing, or boundary
+legality.
+"""
+import sympy as sp
+
+checks=[]
+def check(label, condition):
+    ok=bool(condition); checks.append(ok); print(("PASS " if ok else "FAIL ")+label)
+
+x,y,a,b,c,lam = sp.symbols("x y a b c lambda", real=True)
+L = a*x**2/2 + b*y**2/2 + c*x*y
+Ex, Ey = sp.diff(L,x), sp.diff(L,y)
+
+# Reciprocal hard constraint C=x+y=0, hence y=-x and tangent (dx,dy)=(1,-1).
+Lr = sp.expand(L.subs(y,-x))
+Er = sp.diff(Lr,x)
+check("restrict-then-vary gives tangent projection Ex-Ey", sp.simplify(Er-(Ex-Ey).subs(y,-x))==0)
+normal = sp.simplify((Ex+Ey).subs(y,-x))
+check("a separate normal equation remains", normal != 0)
+
+# Multiplier imposes C=0 but gives only equality Ex=Ey after lambda elimination.
+ELx = Ex+lam
+ELy = Ey+lam
+check("multiplier elimination gives Ex-Ey=0", sp.simplify(ELx-ELy-(Ex-Ey))==0)
+
+# Concrete witness: L=x+y vanishes after restriction, yet both full Euler
+# components are nonzero. Tangent variation and multiplier stationarity allow it.
+Lw=x+y
+Ewx,Ewy=sp.diff(Lw,x),sp.diff(Lw,y)
+check("tangent-stationary witness is not full-stationary", sp.diff(Lw.subs(y,-x),x)==0 and (Ewx!=0 or Ewy!=0))
+check("multiplier can solve witness equations", sp.solve([Ewx+lam,Ewy+lam],[lam], dict=True)==[{lam:-1}])
+
+# Least-squares normal equations are another theory: stationary points of E^2
+# need not be zeros without rank assumptions.
+z=sp.symbols("z", real=True)
+E=sp.Matrix([z**2+1])
+normal_eq=sp.diff((E.dot(E))/2,z)
+check("normal equation can vanish while original equation does not", normal_eq.subs(z,0)==0 and E[0].subs(z,0)!=0)
+
+print("FULL_EULER", Ex, Ey)
+print("TANGENT_EULER", Er)
+print("NORMAL_EULER_ON_CONSTRAINT", normal)
+print("LIMIT: finite chain-rule model only; continuum/gauge/boundary equivalence is not certified")
+print(("PASS" if all(checks) else "FAIL")+f" SUMMARY {sum(checks)}/{len(checks)} checks")
+

apply patch
patch: completed

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Arm-C algebra for total derivatives, boundary momentum, and EH reduction.
+
+Certifies that a total derivative preserves the bulk Euler equation while
+shifting boundary momentum, and that reciprocal-spherical EH is a radial total
+derivative. It cannot select boundary data, corner terms, reference subtraction,
+charge normalization, topology, or a differentiable gravitational action.
+"""
+import sympy as sp
+
+checks=[]
+def check(label, condition):
+    ok=bool(condition); checks.append(ok); print(("PASS " if ok else "FAIL ")+label)
+
+r=sp.symbols("r", positive=True)
+lam=sp.symbols("lambda", real=True)
+f=sp.Function("f")(r)
+fp=sp.diff(f,r)
+L0=fp**2/2
+F=lam*f
+L1=L0+sp.diff(F,r)
+def EL(L):
+    return sp.simplify(sp.diff(L,f)-sp.diff(sp.diff(L,fp),r))
+check("total derivative leaves bulk Euler equation unchanged", sp.simplify(EL(L1)-EL(L0))==0)
+p0,p1=sp.diff(L0,fp),sp.diff(L1,fp)
+check("total derivative shifts boundary momentum", sp.simplify(p1-p0-lam)==0)
+check("Dirichlet parity does not set normal derivative", p0 != 0)
+
+# Reciprocal spherical EH identity for ds2=-A dt2+A^-1 dr2+r2dOmega2.
+A=sp.Function("A")(r)
+R=-sp.diff(A,r,2)-4*sp.diff(A,r)/r+2*(1-A)/r**2
+primitive=-r**2*sp.diff(A,r)-2*r*A+2*r
+check("r^2 R is an exact radial total derivative", sp.simplify(r**2*R-sp.diff(primitive,r))==0)
+
+# Reference subtraction and overall normalization remain independent algebraically.
+q,qref,k=sp.symbols("q q_ref k", real=True)
+Q=k*(q-qref)
+check("reference and normalization are independent charge choices", sp.diff(Q,qref)==-k and sp.diff(Q,k)==q-qref)
+
+print("BOUNDARY_MOMENTUM_BEFORE",p0)
+print("BOUNDARY_MOMENTUM_AFTER",p1)
+print("EH_PRIMITIVE",primitive)
+print("LIMIT: bulk algebra does not select a finite-cell primitive, subtraction, or normalized charge")
+print(("PASS" if all(checks) else "FAIL")+f" SUMMARY {sum(checks)}/{len(checks)} checks")
+

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Arm-C exact carrier convention, covariance, and source-channel audit.
+
+Certifies encoded signs, ordered antisymmetric-index factors, static reduction,
+Weyl exponents, source identities, generic direct-weight chain rules, and a
+same-static-energy time-sector kernel.  It cannot select the carrier, action,
+time variable, representative, weights, measure, variation domain, boundary
+data, or a physical source/mass law.
+"""
+import sympy as sp
+
+checks = []
+def check(label, condition):
+    ok = bool(condition)
+    checks.append(ok)
+    print(("PASS " if ok else "FAIL ") + label)
+
+N, sqh = sp.symbols("N sqrt_h", positive=True)
+xi, k4 = sp.symbols("xi kappa4", real=True)
+Dt2, Xs, E0, B = sp.symbols("Dt2 Xs E0 B", real=True)
+a2, a4 = sp.symbols("a2 a4", real=True)
+
+# (-,+,+,+), zero shift. B and E0 use ordered contractions.
+F2 = B - 2*E0/N**2
+Lcov = sp.expand(-N*sqh*(xi*(-Dt2/N**2 + Xs)/2 + k4*F2/4))
+Lstatic = sp.simplify(Lcov.subs({Dt2: 0, E0: 0}))
+expected = -N*sqh*(xi*Xs/2 + k4*B/4)
+check("corrected covariant action reduces to minus positive static E2+E4", sp.simplify(Lstatic-expected) == 0)
+
+# Matching foliation form has positive electric quartic term.
+Lfol = N*sqh*(a2*Dt2/(2*N**2) - xi*Xs/2 + a4*E0/(2*N**2) - k4*B/4)
+check("covariant/foliation mapping is a2=xi and a4=kappa4", sp.simplify(Lfol.subs({a2:xi,a4:k4})-Lcov) == 0)
+check("time coefficients disappear from static restriction", sp.diff(Lfol.subs({Dt2:0,E0:0}), a2) == 0 and sp.diff(Lfol.subs({Dt2:0,E0:0}), a4) == 0)
+
+# Independent spatial components and ordered-index convention.
+xpar, xp1, xp2 = sp.symbols("xpar xp1 xp2", nonnegative=True)
+f12, f13, f23 = sp.symbols("f12 f13 f23", real=True)
+X = xpar + xp1 + xp2
+Y = 2*(f12**2 + f13**2 + f23**2)
+rho2, rho4 = xi*X/2, k4*Y/4
+S2, S4 = -rho2, rho4
+check("unrestricted metric identity rho+S=2rho4", sp.simplify(rho2+rho4+S2+S4-2*rho4) == 0)
+p2par = xi*(xpar-X/2)
+p4par = k4*(f12**2+f13**2-Y/4)
+rho2par = xi*xpar/2
+rho4par = k4*(f12**2+f13**2)/2
+constrained = sp.simplify(rho2+rho4+p2par+p4par)
+check("reciprocal tangent identity is directional", sp.simplify(constrained-2*(rho2par+rho4par)) == 0)
+check("directional and traced sources are generically unequal", sp.simplify(constrained-2*rho4) != 0)
+
+# Direct position-dependent weights are additional functional freedom.
+phi = sp.symbols("phi", real=True)
+u2p,u2t,u4p,u4t = sp.symbols("u2p u2t u4p u4t")
+b2p,b2t,b4p,b4t = sp.symbols("b2p b2t b4p b4t")
+weighted = (sp.exp((b2p-2)*phi)*u2p + sp.exp(b2t*phi)*u2t
+            + sp.exp((b4p-2)*phi)*u4p + sp.exp(b4t*phi)*u4t)
+source0 = sp.expand(sp.diff(weighted, phi).subs(phi, 0))
+expected_source = (b2p-2)*u2p+b2t*u2t+(b4p-2)*u4p+b4t*u4t
+check("chosen carrier weights change every source coefficient", sp.simplify(source0-expected_source) == 0)
+
+# Weyl weights in d=4 for weight-zero n.
+check("carrier L2 and L4 have different CSN weights", (4-2)==2 and (4-4)==0)
+
+print("F_MUNU_FMUNU", F2)
+print("SOURCE_TRACED", sp.simplify(2*rho4))
+print("SOURCE_DIRECTIONAL", constrained)
+print("SOURCE_DIFFERENCE", sp.factor(constrained-2*rho4))
+print("LIMIT: encoded algebra does not select covariance, weights, variation domain, or mass")
+print(("PASS" if all(checks) else "FAIL") + f" SUMMARY {sum(checks)}/{len(checks)} checks")
+

index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..ca2715c86653c741c72b5f9a1f219af971a19464

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Arm-C independent three-part mass/virial accounting.
+
+Certifies the conditional stress/source identity and bookkeeping implications.
+It cannot prove EH, minimal coupling, a finite-cell mass generator, a local
+surface theorem, the reported numerical gaps, exact criticality, or a
+controlled asymptotic/domain limit.
+"""
+import sympy as sp
+
+checks=[]
+def check(label, condition):
+    ok=bool(condition); checks.append(ok); print(("PASS " if ok else "FAIL ")+label)
+
+E2,E4,B,W=sp.symbols("E2 E4 B_boundary W_res", real=True)
+M0=2*E4
+Ecar=E2+E4
+defect=sp.expand(M0-(Ecar+B+W))
+check("finite-cell virial implies M0=Ecarrier+B+W", sp.simplify(defect.subs(E4,E2+B+W))==0)
+check("exact criticality W=0 still leaves boundary term", sp.simplify((M0-Ecar).subs({E4:E2+B,W:0})-B)==0)
+check("separate B=W=0 closure gives M0=Ecarrier", sp.simplify((M0-Ecar).subs(E4,E2+B+W).subs({B:0,W:0}))==0)
+
+# Exact source identity for the chosen minimal 4D L2+L4 completion.
+rho2,rho4=sp.symbols("rho2 rho4", real=True)
+S2,S4=-rho2,rho4
+check("minimal carrier gives rho+S=2rho4", sp.simplify((rho2+rho4)+(S2+S4)-2*rho4)==0)
+
+# A nonzero reported relative virial gap algebraically forbids E2=E4.
+gap=sp.Rational(-27,1000)
+check("nonzero -2.7 percent gap forbids finite-box E2=E4", gap != 0)
+
+print("FINITE_CELL_DEFECT",defect)
+print("REPORTED_GAP_RATIONAL",gap)
+print("LIMIT: identities are conditional bookkeeping; surface theorem, numerics, and limits remain open")
+print(("PASS" if all(checks) else "FAIL")+f" SUMMARY {sum(checks)}/{len(checks)} checks")
+

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Arm-C exact algebra for the strongest conditional unique-action attempt.
+
+Certifies only Weyl-weight bookkeeping, curvature-basis identities, and the
+finite linear algebra of the C^2/Euler decomposition.  It cannot certify that
+UDT forces metric-only fields, locality, covariance, polynomiality, parity,
+four-derivative minimality, unrestricted variation, a boundary completion, or
+existence/stability of a nontrivial finite-cell solution.
+"""
+import sympy as sp
+
+checks = []
+
+def check(label, condition):
+    ok = bool(condition)
+    checks.append(ok)
+    print(("PASS " if ok else "FAIL ") + label)
+
+d = sp.symbols("d", integer=True, positive=True)
+# Constant-Weyl exponents are necessary checks. Local Weyl transformations add
+# derivative terms to R and therefore make EH even less invariant.
+weights = {
+    "volume": d,
+    "EH": d - 2,
+    "C2": d - 4,
+    "scalar_X2_weight0_phi": d - 4,
+    "S2_quadratic_weight0_n": d - 2,
+    "S2_F2_weight0_n": d - 4,
+}
+at4 = {k: sp.simplify(v.subs(d, 4)) for k, v in weights.items()}
+check("four-dimensional C^2 density has Weyl exponent zero", at4["C2"] == 0)
+check("four-dimensional scalar X^2 density has Weyl exponent zero", at4["scalar_X2_weight0_phi"] == 0)
+check("EH density is not scale neutral", at4["EH"] == 2)
+check("constant-coefficient S2 quadratic density is not scale neutral", at4["S2_quadratic_weight0_n"] == 2)
+check("S2 F^2 density is scale neutral", at4["S2_F2_weight0_n"] == 0)
+
+# Coefficient vectors use the basis (Riemann^2, Ricci^2, R^2).
+C2 = sp.Matrix([1, -2, sp.Rational(1, 3)])
+Euler = sp.Matrix([1, -4, 1])
+p, q = sp.symbols("p q")
+combo = p*C2 + q*Euler
+check("C^2 identity coefficients", C2 == sp.Matrix([1, -2, sp.Rational(1, 3)]))
+check("Euler identity coefficients", Euler == sp.Matrix([1, -4, 1]))
+check("C^2 and Euler are linearly independent", sp.Matrix.hstack(C2, Euler).rank() == 2)
+
+# Modulo the Euler topological vector, the admitted local parity-even
+# curvature-squared Weyl-invariant bulk direction has one coefficient p.
+alpha, beta, gamma = sp.symbols("alpha beta gamma")
+sol = sp.solve(
+    [sp.Eq(alpha, combo[0]), sp.Eq(beta, combo[1]), sp.Eq(gamma, combo[2])],
+    [alpha, beta, gamma], dict=True
+)
+check("conditional curvature-square family is p*C^2+q*Euler", len(sol) == 1)
+check("Euler quotient leaves one bulk normalization p", sp.Matrix.hstack(C2).rank() == 1)
+
+# Lovelock inventory: volume and EH fail even constant local-scale neutrality;
+# Euler is topological in four dimensions and supplies no local metric equation.
+check("EH/Lovelock and exact pre-scale CSN are in tension", at4["volume"] != 0 and at4["EH"] != 0)
+
+print("WEIGHTS_D4", at4)
+print("CURVATURE_COMBINATION", list(combo))
+print("LIMIT: conditional classification only; premises, boundary law, and solutions are not certified")
+print(("PASS" if all(checks) else "FAIL") + f" SUMMARY {sum(checks)}/{len(checks)} checks")
+

new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..68e93b859414a32cc4daebf5e27553bfcae0aefa

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Arm-C finite-dimensional model of vary/restrict/multiplier alternatives.
+
+The calculation certifies the chain-rule loss of the normal Euler equation
+under a hard reciprocal constraint. It is an algebraic analogue, not a proof
+of any continuum action, constraint qualification, gauge fixing, or boundary
+legality.
+"""
+import sympy as sp
+
+checks=[]
+def check(label, condition):
+    ok=bool(condition); checks.append(ok); print(("PASS " if ok else "FAIL ")+label)
+
+x,y,a,b,c,lam = sp.symbols("x y a b c lambda", real=True)
+L = a*x**2/2 + b*y**2/2 + c*x*y
+Ex, Ey = sp.diff(L,x), sp.diff(L,y)
+
+# Reciprocal hard constraint C=x+y=0, hence y=-x and tangent (dx,dy)=(1,-1).
+Lr = sp.expand(L.subs(y,-x))
+Er = sp.diff(Lr,x)
+check("restrict-then-vary gives tangent projection Ex-Ey", sp.simplify(Er-(Ex-Ey).subs(y,-x))==0)
+normal = sp.simplify((Ex+Ey).subs(y,-x))
+check("a separate normal equation remains", normal != 0)
+
+# Multiplier imposes C=0 but gives only equality Ex=Ey after lambda elimination.
+ELx = Ex+lam
+ELy = Ey+lam
+check("multiplier elimination gives Ex-Ey=0", sp.simplify(ELx-ELy-(Ex-Ey))==0)
+
+# Concrete witness: L=x+y vanishes after restriction, yet both full Euler
+# components are nonzero. Tangent variation and multiplier stationarity allow it.
+Lw=x+y
+Ewx,Ewy=sp.diff(Lw,x),sp.diff(Lw,y)
+check("tangent-stationary witness is not full-stationary", sp.diff(Lw.subs(y,-x),x)==0 and (Ewx!=0 or Ewy!=0))
+check("multiplier can solve witness equations", sp.solve([Ewx+lam,Ewy+lam],[lam], dict=True)==[{lam:-1}])
+
+# Least-squares normal equations are another theory: stationary points of E^2
+# need not be zeros without rank assumptions.
+z=sp.symbols("z", real=True)
+E=sp.Matrix([z**2+1])
+normal_eq=sp.diff((E.dot(E))/2,z)
+check("normal equation can vanish while original equation does not", normal_eq.subs(z,0)==0 and E[0].subs(z,0)!=0)
+
+print("FULL_EULER", Ex, Ey)
+print("TANGENT_EULER", Er)
+print("NORMAL_EULER_ON_CONSTRAINT", normal)
+print("LIMIT: finite chain-rule model only; continuum/gauge/boundary equivalence is not certified")
+print(("PASS" if all(checks) else "FAIL")+f" SUMMARY {sum(checks)}/{len(checks)} checks")
+

@@ -0,0 +1,45 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Arm-C algebra for total derivatives, boundary momentum, and EH reduction.
+
+Certifies that a total derivative preserves the bulk Euler equation while
+shifting boundary momentum, and that reciprocal-spherical EH is a radial total
+derivative. It cannot select boundary data, corner terms, reference subtraction,
+charge normalization, topology, or a differentiable gravitational action.
+"""
+import sympy as sp
+
+checks=[]
+def check(label, condition):
+    ok=bool(condition); checks.append(ok); print(("PASS " if ok else "FAIL ")+label)
+
+r=sp.symbols("r", positive=True)
+lam=sp.symbols("lambda", real=True)
+f=sp.Function("f")(r)
+fp=sp.diff(f,r)
+L0=fp**2/2
+F=lam*f
+L1=L0+sp.diff(F,r)
+def EL(L):
+    return sp.simplify(sp.diff(L,f)-sp.diff(sp.diff(L,fp),r))
+check("total derivative leaves bulk Euler equation unchanged", sp.simplify(EL(L1)-EL(L0))==0)
+p0,p1=sp.diff(L0,fp),sp.diff(L1,fp)
+check("total derivative shifts boundary momentum", sp.simplify(p1-p0-lam)==0)
+check("Dirichlet parity does not set normal derivative", p0 != 0)
+
+# Reciprocal spherical EH identity for ds2=-A dt2+A^-1 dr2+r2dOmega2.
+A=sp.Function("A")(r)
+R=-sp.diff(A,r,2)-4*sp.diff(A,r)/r+2*(1-A)/r**2
+primitive=-r**2*sp.diff(A,r)-2*r*A+2*r
+check("r^2 R is an exact radial total derivative", sp.simplify(r**2*R-sp.diff(primitive,r))==0)
+
+# Reference subtraction and overall normalization remain independent algebraically.
+q,qref,k=sp.symbols("q q_ref k", real=True)
+Q=k*(q-qref)
+check("reference and normalization are independent charge choices", sp.diff(Q,qref)==-k and sp.diff(Q,k)==q-qref)
+
+print("BOUNDARY_MOMENTUM_BEFORE",p0)
+print("BOUNDARY_MOMENTUM_AFTER",p1)
+print("EH_PRIMITIVE",primitive)
+print("LIMIT: bulk algebra does not select a finite-cell primitive, subtraction, or normalized charge")
+print(("PASS" if all(checks) else "FAIL")+f" SUMMARY {sum(checks)}/{len(checks)} checks")
+
diff --git a/cas_armc_carrier_covariance.py b/cas_armc_carrier_covariance.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..cbc7817dca4313e0b3e7759638d41c913dfc6fcb
